How to Fill SSC CGL 2025 Exam Online Form

  • OTR Instruction : SSC has started one-time registration on its new website SSC.GOV.IN. Candidates who have not yet done OTR registration on the new website will be able to apply for any recruitment only after completing the registration.
  • Staff Selection Commission SSC Are Issued Combined Graduate Level CGL Exam 2025 Notification and Invited Online Application Form for the SSC Graduate Level Recruitment 2025 Candidate Can Apply Online for 09/06/2025 to 04/07/2025.
  • Photo Instruction : SSC has made a big change in the application process. Now the photo of the candidate will be uploaded live, that too through webcam or through the official SSC App. The candidate has to take care that he looks straight ahead in the photo and the background should also be light/white.
  • Candidate Read the Notification Before Apply the Staff Selection Commission SSC CGL Career Latest Various Graduate Level Recruitment 2025. Application Form in OnlyJobs Recruitment Latest Job Section.
  • Kindly Check and Collect the All Document - Hand Writing, Eligibility, ID Proof, Address Details, Basic Details.
  • Kindly Ready Scan Document Related to Recruitment Form - Photo, Sign, ID, Thumb, Proof, Etc.
  • Before Submit the Application Form Must Check the Preview and All Column Carefully.
  • If Candidate Required to Paying the Application Fee Must Submit. If You have Not the Required Application Fees Your Form is Not Completed.
  • Take A Print Out of Final Submitted Form.

Interested Candidates Can Read the SSC CGL 2025 Notification Before Apply Online

How to Fill UPSSSC Rajasva Lekhpal Online Form 2022

  • There are 02 types to apply online for UPSSSC Rajasva Lekhpal Recruitment 2022.
  • First: In this, the candidate will have to login with his personal information, the information that has to be given is: PET Registration Number, Date of Birth, Gender, Domicile and Category.
  • Second : In this, the candidate will have to login through his OTP, the information that has to be given is: PET Registration Number and OTP Password.
  • After login, the complete information of the candidate, his photo and signature will also be visible, the candidate will have to fill the information related to the post he is asking for and the application fee: Rs.25/- will have to be paid.
  • Kindly Check and Collect the All Document - Eligibility, ID Proof, Address Details, Basic Details.
  • Kindly Ready Scan Document Related to Recruitment Form - Photo, Sign, ID Proof, Etc.
  • Before Apply Online / Submit Application Form Must Check the Preview and All Column Carefully.
  • Take A Print Out of Final Submitted Form.

OnlyJobs® : DSSSB Result 2026 How to Check

  • DSSSB (Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board) exam results can be checked easily through OnlyJobs®. Candidates searching for OnlyJobs DSSSB Result 2026 can follow the methods below to check their result, marks, and cutoff details.
  • There are two main methods to check DSSSB results – first through PDF and second by logging into the DSSSB online portal.
  • First : DSSSB Result PDF Method
    In many cases, DSSSB releases the result in PDF format. Candidates need to download the PDF and search their roll number using the Ctrl + F option. If the roll number is found in the list, the candidate is considered qualified.
  • Second : DSSSB Online Portal Login Method
    Some results are available through the DSSSB online portal where candidates can check their scorecard, marks, and detailed result.
  • To login, candidates need to enter:
  • Date of Birth
  • Class 10 Roll Number
  • Passing Year
  • Password created during the application process
  • After logging in, click on the “View Result” tab to check your result and marks.
For the latest updates on DSSSB Result, Admit Card, Answer Key, and Cutoff, always visit OnlyJobs, the official website for OnlyJobs® in India (Since 2012).

DSSSB Important Note (Qualified Candidate)

  • Candidates who qualify in the DSSSB examination may be required to fill the E-Dossier Form. Complete details will be available in the official result or cutoff notice.

Enrolled Candidate Can Download the Delhi DSSSB Latest Result 2026.

Application Fee

  • For Single Paper :
  • General / OBC / EWS: 1000/-
  • SC / ST / PH : 500/-
  • For Both Paper Primary / Junior :
  • General / OBC / EWS: 1200/-
  • SC / ST / PH : 600/-
  • Pay the Examination Fee Through Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking or E Challan

CBSE CTET February 2026 Exam Eligibility Code Details

CTET Primary Level (Class I to V): Eligibility with Code February 2026
  1. Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing of 2- year Diploma in Elementary Education (by whatever name known) OR
  2. Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 45% marks and passed or appearing in of 2- year Diploma in Elementary Education (by whatever name known), in accordance with the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure), Regulations, 2002.  OR
  3. Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in 4- year Bachelor of Elementary Education (B.El.Ed).
  4. Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in 2- year Diploma in Education (Special Education).
  5. More Eligibility Details Read the Notification

CTET Junior Level (Class VI to VIII): Eligibility with Code February 2026
  1. Graduation and passed or appearing in final year of 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education (by whatever name known).
  2. At least 50% marks either in Graduation or in Post-Graduation and passed or appearing in Bachelor in Education (B.Ed).
  3. Graduation with at least 45% marks and passed or appearing in Bachelor in Education (B.Ed), in accordance with the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure) Regulations issued from time to time in this regard.
  4. Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in of 4- year Bachelor in Elementary Education (B.El.Ed).
  5. Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in of 4- year B.A/B.Sc.Ed or B.A.Ed/B.Sc.Ed.
  6. Graduation with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in  B.Ed. (Special Education)*.
  7. Any candidate having qualified B.Ed. Programme recognized by the NCTE is eligible to appear in TET/CTET. Moreover, as per the existing TET guidelines circulated vide NCTE letter dated 11-02-2011, a person who is pursuing any of the teacher education courses (recognized by the NCTE or the RCI, as the case may be) specified in the NCTE Notification dated 23rd August 2010 is also qualified to appear in the TET/CTET.
  8. Post-Graduation with a minimum 55% marks or equivalent grade and passed or appearing in three-year integrated B.Ed.-M.Ed.

DSHM Pharmacist Result 2026 OUT (Direct Link) - Download Selected List

Quick Summary: Delhi State Health Mission (DSHM) has released DSHM Pharmacist Result 2026 on 10-04-2026 at official portal dshm.delhi.gov.in. Candidates can check the final selected list for PM-ABHIM Pharmacist posts (200 vacancies filled). Check category-wise selection, merit details, and joining process below.

Are you waiting for DSHM Pharmacist Result 2026? Great news! The Delhi State Health Mission has officially published the final result for Pharmacist posts under Pradhan Mantri Ayushman Bharat Health Infrastructure Mission (PM-ABHIM) on contractual basis on 10-04-2026. Thousands of candidates who appeared in the interview held from 26-02-2026 to 11-03-2026 at Integrated District Health Societies under DSHM can now verify their selection status online.

This article provides complete information about DSHM Pharmacist Result 2026 including direct download links, selected candidates list, category-wise summary, and important instructions for further joining process.

Note: All 200 vacancies have been filled as per the final selection. The list includes candidates from UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS and a few UR-PH categories.

How to Check DSHM Pharmacist Result 2026?

Candidates can download the DSHM Pharmacist Result 2026 PDF by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: dshm.delhi.gov.in
  2. Go to the "Recruitment" or "Notices" section
  3. Find the link for "Final result for the post of Pharmacist for PM-ABHIM"
  4. Click on the PDF link and download it
  5. Search your Registration No. or Name in the selected candidates list

DSHM Pharmacist Selected Candidates List 2026 - What's Inside?

The DSHM Pharmacist final result 2026 PDF contains the complete list of 200 selected candidates with the following details:

  • S.No.

  • Applicant Name

Quick Summary: Mizoram Public Service Commission (MPSC) has released Mizoram PSC Assistant Accounts Officer, Assistant Audit & Accounts Officer & Field Facilitator Shortlisted Candidates List 2026 on 10 April 2026 at official portal mpsc.mizoram.gov.in. Candidates can check their shortlist status using Roll Number in the PDF. Check screening schedule, Google Form option submission, and next steps below.

Are you waiting for Mizoram PSC AAO & AA&AO Result 2026? Great news! The Mizoram Public Service Commission has officially published the shortlisted candidates list for further screening on 10 April 2026. Candidates who appeared in the Common Competitive Examination held on 20 November 2025 for Assistant Accounts Officer (Treasury & Works), Assistant Audit & Accounts Officer under Finance Department and Field Facilitator under SW,W&CD Department can now verify their status online.

This article provides complete information about Mizoram PSC Shortlisted Candidates List 2026 including direct PDF link, roll numbers, category-wise lists, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

Mizoram PSC AAO 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Mizoram Public Service Commission (MPSC)
Post Name Assistant Accounts Officer (Treasury & Works), Assistant Audit & Accounts Officer (Finance Dept.), Field Facilitator (SW,W&CD Dept.)
Result Type Shortlisted Candidates for Document Screening & Personal Interview
Result Declaration Date 10 April 2026
Exam Conducted On 20 November 2025
Selection Stages Written Exam → Document Screening → Personal Interview
Official Website mpsc.mizoram.gov.in

Check Your Shortlist Now

Download Mizoram PSC Shortlisted List 2026

How to Check Mizoram PSC Shortlisted Candidates List 2026?

Candidates can check their Mizoram PSC AAO Shortlist 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: https://mpsc.mizoram.gov.in
  2. Click on “Notifications” or “Results” section
  3. Find and open the PDF: “Shortlisted candidates for further screening...”
  4. Download the PDF
  5. Press Ctrl + F and search your Roll Number
  6. Check your name, father’s name and permanent address

Mizoram PSC Shortlisted Candidates List 2026 - What's Inside?

The Mizoram PSC Shortlist 2026 contains details of candidates shortlisted for document screening and Personal Interview. Names are arranged in order of Roll Numbers only.

Shortlist Contains:

  • Sl. No.
  • Roll Number
  • Name & Father’s Name
  • Permanent Address
  • Category (General / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen)

Types of Lists Released:

  1. General Candidates: Main list (143 candidates)
  2. PwBD (Deaf & hard of hearing): 10 candidates
  3. PwBD (Locomotor): 4 candidates
  4. Ex-Servicemen: 10 candidates

Important Instructions for Shortlisted Candidates

  • ✓ Shortlisted candidates must mandatorily submit Post/Department preference through Google Form (link will be provided)
  • ✓ Last date to submit option: 20 April 2026
  • ✓ Option once exercised shall be final
  • ✓ Only candidates who submit Google Form and have complete documents will be called for Personal Interview
  • ✓ Document screening will verify eligibility criteria as per advertisement
  • ✓ Keep checking official website for Personal Interview schedule
  • ✓ Failure to submit option will lead to exclusion from the process

India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 OUT (Direct Link) - Download State-Wise PDF @indiapostgdsonline.gov.in

Quick Summary: Department of Posts (India Post) has released India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 on 10 April 2026 at official portal indiapostgdsonline.gov.in / indiapost.gov.in/gdsonlineengagement. Candidates can check their selection status using Registration Number in state-wise/circle-wise PDFs. Check qualifying details, document verification schedule, and next steps below.

Are you waiting for India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026? Great news! The Department of Posts has officially published the supplementary 2nd Merit List for Gramin Dak Sevak (GDS) recruitment today on 10 April 2026. Thousands of candidates who applied for BPM, ABPM, and Dak Sevak posts (total 28,635+ vacancies) can now verify their selection status online. Lists for Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal are withheld due to Model Code of Conduct.

This article provides complete information about India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 including direct state-wise PDF links, how to check selection status, merit list details, and step-by-step instructions.

India Post GDS 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Department of Posts, India Post
Post Name Gramin Dak Sevak (GDS) - BPM, ABPM, Dak Sevak
Result Type 2nd Supplementary Merit List
Result Declaration Date 10 April 2026
Application Period 31 January to 14 February 2026
1st Merit List Date 06 March 2026
Selection Stages Merit based on Class 10 Marks → Document Verification → Appointment
Official Website indiapostgdsonline.gov.in / indiapost.gov.in/gdsonlineengagement

Check Your 2nd Merit List Now

Direct Link to Download India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026

Direct official portal - State-wise / Circle-wise PDFs available

How to Check India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026?

Candidates can check their India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: https://indiapost.gov.in/gdsonlineengagement or https://indiapostgdsonline.gov.in/
  2. Go to “Candidate’s Corner” or “Online Engagement” section
  3. Click on the **2nd Merit List / Supplementary List - II** link
  4. Select your Postal Circle / State from the list
  5. Download the PDF
  6. Press Ctrl + F and search your 7-digit **Registration Number**
  7. Check your name, marks, division, post, and document verification details

India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 contains details of newly shortlisted candidates for remaining vacancies. Separate supplementary lists are released circle-wise.

Merit List Contains:

  • Registration Number
  • Candidate Name
  • Division / Office Name
  • Post ID & Post Name (BPM / ABPM / Dak Sevak)
  • Class 10 Marks (calculated to 4 decimal places)
  • Category
  • Document Verification Date & Venue
  • Required Documents List

Types of Lists Released:

  1. Supplementary List - II (2nd Merit List): Released on 10 April 2026
  2. Circle-wise PDFs: Available for 19 circles (Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu & West Bengal withheld)
  3. Further Supplementary Lists: May be released if vacancies remain

Important Instructions for Selected Candidates

  • ✓ Download and save the merit list PDF immediately
  • ✓ Attend Document Verification on the given date at the Divisional Office with all originals + 2 sets of photocopies
  • ✓ Required documents: Class 10 Marksheet & Certificate, ID Proof, Category Certificate (if applicable), Domicile Certificate, Photographs, etc.
  • ✓ Selection is purely provisional; final appointment depends on successful DV and verification
  • ✓ Keep checking the official portal regularly for further updates or additional lists
  • ✓ No waiting list; vacancies are filled through subsequent merit lists

NIACL AO Final Selection 2026 - Information Breakdown

The final selection is based on combined performance in Mains Exam and Interview. Your NIACL AO Final Result 2026 confirms provisional selection subject to medical fitness and document verification.

Are you waiting for IBPS RRB PO Final Result 2026? Great news! Thousands of candidates who appeared in the mains exam and interview across various RRBs can now verify their provisional allotment status online.

This article provides complete information about IBPS RRB PO Final Result 2026 including direct download links, merit list, cutoff marks, scorecard details, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

How to Check IBPS RRB PO Final Result 2026?

Candidates can download their IBPS RRB PO Final Result 2026 scorecard by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: www.ibps.in
  2. Go to the "CRP RRBs XIV" or "Current Openings/Results" section.
  3. Click on the link for "Result for CRP RRBs-XIV (Provisional Allotment)" or "Final Result Officers Scale-I/II/III".
  4. Enter your Registration Number/Roll Number and Password/Date of Birth.
  5. Submit and view your provisional allotment status, final marks, and download the scorecard/PDF.
  6. Save and print for future reference.

IBPS RRB PO Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The IBPS RRB PO merit list 2026 is a comprehensive document containing details of all provisionally allotted candidates. IBPS prepares separate merit lists for different categories and scales.

Merit List Contains:

  • Registration Number/Roll Number of allotted candidates
  • Candidate Name
  • Category (Gen/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Combined Final Marks (Mains 80% + Interview 20%)
  • Provisional Allotment Status & Allotted RRB
  • Rank in merit
  • Qualifying Status

Types of Merit Lists Released:

  1. Overall Provisional Allotment List: All selected candidates
  2. Category-Wise Merit List: Separate for OBC, SC, ST, EWS, etc.
  3. Scale-Wise Lists: For Officer Scale-I, II, and III

IBPS RRB PO Scorecard 2026 - Information Breakdown

Your IBPS RRB PO scorecard 2026 will display the following information:

Candidate Name As per application
Registration/Roll Number Unique ID
Combined Marks (Mains + Interview) Normalized scores
Final Score Out of maximum
Qualifying Status/Allotment Allotted/Not Allotted
Category Rank & Allotted RRB Your position and bank

Important Instructions for Qualified Candidates

  • ✓ Download and save your scorecard immediately
  • ✓ Take 3-4 printouts for future reference
  • ✓ Check email/SMS for allotment details
  • ✓ Prepare for document verification at allotted RRB
  • ✓ Keep all original documents ready (ID proof, certificates, etc.)
  • ✓ Verify personal details - report discrepancies to IBPS/RRB promptly
  • ✓ Join the allotted RRB as per schedule

The SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 has been released by the Staff Selection Commission (SSC).

As per the latest updates, the final marks and scorecard were made available on February 17, 2026, at the official website ssc.gov.in. This follows the declaration of the SSC GD final result on January 15, 2026.

Candidates who appeared in the PST/PET and DV/DME/RME stages can now download their SSC GD final scorecard 2025 using their registration number and password. The scorecard displays normalized CBT marks and overall qualifying status after all recruitment stages. The download window is limited - typically a short period - so act quickly before the link deactivates.

Key Highlights:

  • Release Date: February 17, 2026
  • Last Date to Download: March 1, 2026 (till 6:00 PM)
  • Access: Online only via ssc.gov.in login
  • Purpose: View final marks, compare with cut-offs, and save for records/joining

Final Result Declaration January 15, 2026

Direct Link to Check SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Candidates can access the SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 through the direct link on the SSC portal under the “Result/Marks” section after logging in. The portal may redirect from legacy links, but use ssc.gov.in primarily.

SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 – Direct Link (Active)
(Login required at ssc.gov.in for marks download)

Step-by-Step Guide to Download SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  1. Visit the official Staff Selection Commission website: ssc.gov.in.
  2. Click on the “Login” or “Candidate Login” button on the homepage.
  3. Enter your registration number/username, password, and captcha (if prompted).
  4. After logging in, navigate to the “Result/Marks” tab on your dashboard.
  5. Select “Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, NIA and Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles Examination 2025” from the list.
  6. Click on the “SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025” or “Marks” link to view your details.
  7. Your final scorecard will display on screen - review marks, cut-off comparison, and status.
  8. Download the PDF and print it for future reference (e.g., document verification). Save it promptly, as access ends after the deadline.
  9. (Use the direct link mentioned above to reach the login faster if available.)

Details Mentioned on SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Your scorecard typically includes:

  • Candidate’s name, roll number, registration number
  • Category/sub-category (UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, etc.)
  • Exam name and applied post
  • Section-wise marks (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & General Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi)
  • Raw and normalized marks
  • Total marks in CBT (considered for final merit after qualifying PET/PST and medical)
  • Qualifying status (selected/not selected) and any remarks
  • Note on limited availability window

SSC GD Final Cut Off and Merit List 2025

The SSC GD final cut off 2025 was released with the final result on January 15, 2026, and is force-wise, state-wise, category-wise, and gender-wise. It appears in the official result PDF write-up.

Your scorecard marks should be compared to the relevant cut-off for your category/force/state to assess selection. The merit list PDF lists roll numbers of provisionally selected candidates (around 50,047 selected against vacancies), while the scorecard provides individual marks for those who reached physical/medical stages.

(Refer to the official SSC result PDFs for exact cut-off tables - examples range from ~104 to 152+ depending on category/force.)

What to Do After Downloading SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Selected candidates: Keep multiple printouts/PDFs of the scorecard and final result for document verification, joining, and force allocation.

Verify details (name, roll number, category, etc.) - report serious discrepancies to SSC regional offices promptly.

Non-selected candidates: Use marks for performance analysis and preparation for future SSC exams.

Download and save copies early - the window closes after the last date.

SSC GD Selection Process 2025 (Short Overview)

The process included:

  • Computer-Based Test (CBT): Four sections totaling 160 marks.
  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Physical Standard Test (PST): Qualifying.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME)/Review Medical Examination (RME): Qualifying.
  • Final merit based on normalized CBT marks (for qualified candidates), followed by force/state allocation per vacancies and preferences.

The final scorecard reflects CBT performance post-qualifying stages, available briefly post-result.

  • SSC GD Final Cut Off 2025 PDF: In final result write-up on ssc.gov.in
  • SSC Official Website: ssc.gov.in

Go to psc.ap.gov.in and navigate to the “Results” or “Latest Notifications” section on the homepage.

Click on the notification titled “Selection Notification – Group-II Services (Advt. No. 11/2023)” dated 28.01.2026 or similar link.

Step 3: Download PDF

The selection list PDF will open in a new window/tab.

Step 4: Search Your Details

Use Ctrl + F to search your Roll Number, Hall Ticket Number, or Name in the PDF.

Step 5: Save & Print

Download the PDF and take printouts for future reference/appointment process.

Central Bank of India Faculty 2026 - Key Information Breakdown

Faculty positions at RSETIs are contractual engagements for training rural youth in self-employment skills. Typical requirements include:

Qualification Graduate/Post Graduate (preference for MSW/MA in RD/Psychology/Sociology, B.Ed., teaching experience)
Age Limit Usually 22-40/45 years (relaxation applicable)
Consolidated Remuneration Approx. ₹30,000 per month (varies by notification)
Application Mode Offline (as per regional notification)
Next Step Attend Interview on 17 March 2026

  • ✓ Keep checking official website for any changes or additional notices
  • ✓ If not shortlisted, look for other RSETI/FLC vacancies on the site
  • ✓ Report any issues to the Regional Office, Cooch Behar

SSC GD Constable Exam Pattern 2026

Understanding the exam pattern is crucial for effective preparation. Here's what candidates need to know about the SSC GD Constable examination:

  • Exam Mode: Online Computer Based Test
  • Total Marks: 160
  • Number of Questions: 80
  • Duration: 60 Minutes
  • Negative Marking: 0.50 marks for each wrong answer
  • Subjects/Sections: General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & General Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi

Preparation Tips for SSC GD Constable Exam

With the exam date announced, candidates should focus on smart preparation strategies:

1. Create a Study Schedule: Divide your time wisely among all subjects based on your strengths and weaknesses.

2. Focus on Previous Year Papers: Solve at least 5-10 previous year question papers to understand the pattern and difficulty level.

3. Take Mock Tests: Regular mock tests will help you manage time and identify areas needing improvement.

4. Revise Regularly: Make short notes and revise them daily, especially for GK and current affairs.

5. Stay Updated: Keep yourself updated with current affairs, especially related to CAPFs recruitment.

What After the SSC GD Constable Exam?

After successfully appearing in the SSC GD Constable examination, candidates should:

1. Result Declaration: The result is typically declared within 30-45 days after the examination. Results will be available on the official website.

2. Answer Key: Preliminary answer keys may be released within 2-3 days after the exam. Candidates can raise objections if any discrepancy is found.

3. Next Stage: Qualified candidates will be called for Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Physical Standard Test (PST), Medical Examination & Document Verification as per the selection process.

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):

  • Last Date for Registration : NA

Application Fee

  • General / OBC / EWS : 0/-
  • SC / ST : 0/-

SSC OTR Eligibility

  • One time registration is necessary only for those who want to appear in any SSC recruitment in future. With the help of this registration, the candidate will save a lot of time while applying.
  • No Age Limit in SSC OTR Registration.

SSC Popular Vacancy 

  • SSC Combined Graduate Level CGL Exam
  • SSC Multi Tasking Staff MTS Exam
  • SSC Selection Post Exam
  • SSC Constable GD Recruitment Exam
  • SSC Delhi Police Constable Recruitment Exam
  • SSC Delhi Police Sub Inspector Exam
  • SSC CPO SI Recruitment Exam
  • SSC CHSL 10+2 Recruitment Exam
  • SSC Stenographer Exam
  • SSC Junior Engineer JE Exam
  • SSC Junior Hindi Translator JHT Exam

How to Apply SSC One Time Registration OTR Online Form 2025

  • Staff Selection Commission SSC issued a notice on 22 February 2024 saying that they have launched a new website SSC Gov in, in which all the candidates will have to do fresh one time registration (OTR)
  • Step 1 : Open the Website ssc.gov.in or Direct Link Will be in this Page Some Useful Important Links Section
  • Step 2 : Click on New Registration Tab
  • Step 3 : Students will have to give their basic information such as: Name, Father's Name, Mother's Name, Mobile Number, Email Id and High School Class 10 Roll Number.
  • Step 4 : Verify Your OTR Profile Details & Submit Registration.
  • Step 5 : An OTP will be received on the registered email and registered mobile number of the candidate, he will have to enter it.
  • Step 6 : The registration Part I process will be completed, the candidate will see the OTR Registration No. on the screen.
  • Step 7 : In the next step, you will have to change the default password which has been received on your email, after that you will have to login by writing the registration number along with the password which has been received on the email.
  • Step 9 : Candidates should change the default password of OTR registration number with their custom password. Use capital letters, small letters, numbers and special characters while creating the new password which should be at least 08 digits. Additionally, two security questions will also have to be selected, so that if you forget your password in future, you can recover your password with the help of the security question.
  • Step 10 :  After changing the password, the candidate will have to login again, after that the information about his nationality, category, Visible Identification Mark and information about permanent and local address will be entered in the form.
  • Step 11 : The last step is that here you have to first double check all the information by clicking on the preview button of OTR, after checking, if there is any error then correct it, otherwise click on the declare button and submit the form finally.
  • Step 12 : The registration process of SSC OTR has now been completed, the candidate will be able to see the dashboard whenever any new recruitment comes, or the candidate can also get the information about admit card and result by logging into the SSC dashboard.
  • Our team has also shown a video on how to do SSC one time registration, which you can watch and do your new registration by following the step by step process: SSC OTR Registration Kaise Kare Video Dekhe
  • For more information, you have to complete the notice issued to SSC OTR.

Post Date: 09-04-2026

Latest Update: 10-04-2026

Brief Information: The Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) has announced a notification for the recruitment of 9195 Constable Tradesman Vacancy. Eligible Candidates who are interested in the vacancy details & completed all eligibility criteria can read the notification & Apply.

CRPF Constable Tradesman Recruitment 2026 Overview

The Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) has officially released a recruitment notification for Constable Tradesman . For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF)

Constable Tradesman Vacancy 2026

WWW.OnlyJobs

Application Fee

  • For General, OBC, EWS :Rs. 100/- (for male candidates of General, EWS & OBC only)
  • Exempted for SC, ST, Female (all categories) and Ex-servicemen candidates

Note: Applications received without prescribed fee (where applicable) shall be summarily rejected. No hard copy of application is required to be sent.

CRPF Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

  • Notification Release Date: 08-04-2026
  • Starting Date for Apply Online: 20-04-2026
  • Last Date for Apply Online: 19-05-2026

CRPF Recruitment 2026 Age Limit (as on 01-01-2026)

  • Age Limit for Constable (Driver): 21-27 Years
  • Constable (Motor Mechanic Vehicle / Cobbler / Carpenter / Tailor / Brass Band / Pipe Band / Gardner / Bugler / Painter / Cook / Barber cum Hair Dresser / Washer Man & Women / Water Carrier / Safai Karamchari / Mason / Plumber / Electrician): 18-23 Years
  • Age relaxation is applicable as per rules.

Qualification

  • Driver: Matriculation + Heavy Transport Vehicle Driving License
  • Motor Mechanic Vehicle: Matriculation + 2 years ITI in Mechanic Motor Vehicle + 1 year experience
  • All other Tradesmen: Matriculation + proficient in respective trade
  • Pioneer Wing (Mason/Electrician): Matriculation + 1 year experience in trade (ITI preferred)

Pay Scale

  • Pay Level-3: ₹21,700 - 69,100

CRPF Constable Tradesman Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details

Constable ( Tradesman & Technical) 9175 
Constable Tradesman (Pioneer Wing) 20
Interested Candidates Can Read the Full Notification Before Apply Online
Important Links
Apply Online Click Here
Notification Click here
Official Website Click here
OnlyJobs
Join Telegram Channel
Download Mobile App
Join WhatsApp Channel

Selection Process

The recruitment process will consist of Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Physical Standards Test (PST), Computer Based Test (CBT), Trade/Skill Test, Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination (DME/RME).

  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Qualifying in nature.
    • For Constable (Driver, Motor Mechanic Vehicle, Gardner, Painter, Carpenter, Brass Band, Pipe Band, Cobbler, Tailor & Bugler): Male – 5 Kms race in 24 minutes; Female – 1.6 Kms in 8.30 minutes.
    • For Constable (Cook/Water Carrier, Barber & Hair Dresser, Washer man & Washer women, Safai Karmachari): Male – 1.6 Kms in 10 minutes; Female – 1.6 Kms in 12 minutes.
    • For Pioneer Wing (Mason / Electrician): Male – 1.6 Kms in 09 minutes.
    • Pregnant women candidates will be declared temporarily unfit.
  • Physical Standards Test (PST): Height, Chest (for males only) & Weight (proportionate to height & age).
    • Height: Male – 170 Cms (minimum); Female – 157 Cms (minimum). Relaxations applicable for certain categories (ST, NE States, Gorkhas, etc.).
    • Chest (Male only): Un-expanded – 80 Cms, Minimum expansion – 05 Cms. Relaxations applicable.
    • Candidates failing PST will be eliminated.
  • Computer Based Test (CBT): 100 marks (100 MCQs), duration 02 hours.
    • General Intelligence & Reasoning – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • General Knowledge & General Awareness – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • Elementary Mathematics – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • English/Hindi – 25 Qs / 25 Marks
    • Negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer.
    • Qualifying marks: UR – 30%, EWS/OBC – 25%, SC/ST & others – 20%.
    • Marks will be normalized. Merit list prepared on CBT marks.
  • Trade/Skill Test: Qualifying in nature (50 marks, minimum 20 marks required). Practical assessment of the applied trade/post.
  • Document Verification (DV): Original documents (Matric certificate, Domicile/PRC, Trade certificates, Caste/EWS/ESM certificates, etc.) will be verified.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME) / Review Medical Examination (RME): As per Uniform Guidelines issued by MHA. Temporary unfitness allowed only for pregnancy. Final selection subject to medical fitness.
  • Final merit list prepared on the basis of marks obtained in CBT (subject to qualifying all stages). Separate merit lists for male/female and each State/UT (All India basis for Pioneer posts).

How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through CRPF website. No other mode will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website www.rect.crpf.gov.in.
  2. Click on the link for “Recruitment for the post of Constable (Technical & Tradesmen and Pioneer) 2026”.
  3. Register by providing basic details and generate Provisional Registration Number & Password.
  4. Fill the online application form carefully (personal details, educational qualification, trade/post applied, State/UT of domicile, etc.).
  5. Upload scanned copies of recent colour passport size photograph, signature, left thumb impression and handwritten declaration as per specifications.
  6. Pay the application fee online (if applicable) through BHIM, UPI, Net Banking, Debit/Credit Card or CSC Wallet.
  7. Submit the application and take printout of the application form & e-receipt for future reference.
  8. Candidates should bring two coloured printouts of the Admit Card at every stage of examination.

General Instructions

  • Recruitment is domicile-based for Technical & Tradesmen posts and All India basis for Pioneer posts. Candidates must submit valid Domicile/Permanent Residence Certificate of the applied State/UT at DV/DME.
  • Candidates must be citizens of India.
  • Disqualification: Candidates who have entered into or contracted marriage with a person having a spouse living or who, having a spouse living, have entered into or contracted marriage with any person (subject to personal law exemptions).
  • Age & educational qualification will be reckoned as on 01-01-2026.
  • Only Indian Nationals need to apply. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in PET/PST/CBT/Trade Test/DV/DME.
  • Mere submission of application or issue of Admit Card does not confer any right to selection.
  • The CRPF reserves the right to cancel/restrict/enlarge/modify the recruitment process without assigning any reason.
  • Any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Courts having jurisdiction over the Examination Centres.
  • All correspondence will be made only through the registered email ID/mobile number provided by the candidate.
  • Candidates are advised to visit www.rect.crpf.gov.in regularly for updates.
  • For any queries, candidates may contact CRPF Helpline No. 011-26160255.

Other Terms and Conditions

  • You can take the Aadhaar Supervisor exam through this link: https://uidai.nseitexams.com/UIDAI/LoginAction_input.action
  • When you apply for the exam, an authorization request is sent to the State Team.
  • Once they approve the request, you will be able to appear for the Aadhaar Supervisor exam.
  • You can take the Aadhaar LMS Certification once your LMS ID and Password are created and approved. After that, you will be able to appear for the LMS Certification through this link:   https://e-learning.uidai.gov.in

How to Apply for CSC e‑Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Recruitment 2026

Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online only through the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website. Follow the steps given below to submit your application form.

Visit Official Website

  • Go to the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website: csc.gov.in.

  • On the homepage, navigate to the Recruitment/Careers/Latest Opening section.

Open Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Application Link

  • Find and click on the notification/link titled “CSC e‑Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Recruitment 2026” or similar.

  • Click on the Apply Online button to proceed to the application page.

Registration (New Users)

  • Click on New Registration (if applying for the first time).

  • Enter basic details such as Name, Mobile Number, Email ID, Date of Birth, and create a password.

  • Submit the details to generate your Registration ID and Password (note these for future login).

Login and Fill Application Form

  • Login using your Registration ID and Password.

  • Fill in the online application form carefully with personal details, contact details, educational qualification, Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator certificate details, and preferred state/district.

  • Ensure that all details match your certificates and Aadhaar data.

Upload Documents

  • Upload scanned copies of the following documents in the prescribed format and size (as mentioned in the notification):

    • Recent passport size photograph

    • Signature

    • 10th/12th/ITI/Polytechnic Diploma certificates and mark sheets

    • Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certificate issued by UIDAI‑authorized Testing & Certifying Agency

    • Any other documents required as per notification

  • Preview the uploaded documents and confirm that they are clear and readable.

Application Fee Payment (If Applicable)

  • Check the Application Fee section in the official notification.

  • If any fee is prescribed, pay it online through available modes such as Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI.

  • After successful payment, download/print the fee receipt.

Final Submission

  • Carefully review all the details filled in the application form in the Preview section.

  • If required, make corrections before final submission.

  • Click on Final Submit to complete the application process.

Print Application Form

  • After successful submission, download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for future reference.

  • Keep the Registration ID, Password, and printout safe for further recruitment stages.

Selection Process

Selection will be made through Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by Scrutiny/Verification of Documents and Medical Examination.

  • Computer Based Test (CBT):
    • HEMM Operator (Trainee) (all posts): Common paper – 100 Marks, 90 minutes duration.
    • Paramedical Staff & Overseer (Civil): Separate paper for each post – 100 Marks (Section-A: 70 Marks Technical + Section-B: 30 Marks GK/Reasoning/Aptitude), 90 minutes duration.
    • No penalty for wrong answers. No marks for un-attempted questions.
    • Bilingual (English & Hindi). In case of any ambiguity in Hindi, English version will be final.
    • Minimum Qualifying Marks: UR/EWS – 50 marks; SC/ST/ESM/OBC(NCL)/PwBD – 40 marks.
    • Tie-breaking rules prescribed for each category of posts (matric marks, DOB, Section-A marks, alphabetical order etc.).
    • Indicative syllabus given in Annexure-A, B & C of the notification.
  • Document Scrutiny / Verification: Only candidates who qualify CBT as per cut-off will be called for verification of original documents/certificates in order of merit.
  • Medical Examination: Selected candidates must be found medically fit as per CIL Medical Attendance Rules.
  • Final selection is provisional and subject to verification of all documents, character & antecedents, and medical fitness.
  • Result and provisional merit panels will be published on NCL website.

How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through the official website https://www.nclcil.in (Career > Recruitment > Apply Online). No other mode accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit https://www.nclcil.in → Career → Recruitment → Employment Notice/Advertisement for Direct Recruitment → Apply Online.
  2. Register and fill the online application form carefully with all mandatory details.
  3. Upload scanned copies of: Recent passport size photograph, signature, Matriculation certificate, HMV/Transport License (for HEMM posts), relevant Diploma/Degree/Registration certificates, Caste/EWS/PwBD/ESM certificates (if applicable).
  4. Pay the application fee online (if applicable).
  5. Submit the application and take a printout for future reference.
  6. Keep checking the NCL website regularly for updates, admit card, result etc.

General Instructions

  • Candidates must read the complete Employment Notice/Advertisement carefully before applying.
  • Cut-off date for determining eligibility (age, qualification, reservation etc.) is 01-05-2026.
  • Minimum/Essential qualifications acquired through distance learning/part-time shall not be considered.
  • Reservation & relaxation benefits will be given as per Govt. of India rules. Candidates must possess valid certificates in prescribed format.
  • All future correspondence/updates (admit card, result, notices etc.) will be available only on NCL website www.nclcil.in. Candidates are advised to visit the website regularly.
  • Mere issuance of admit card does not guarantee eligibility. Candidature remains provisional till document verification.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • Decision of NCL Management in all matters relating to eligibility, selection etc. shall be final and binding.
  • Court of jurisdiction for any dispute will be Hon’ble MP High Court, Jabalpur (M.P.).
  • Selected candidates can be posted in any mine/establishment/hospital of NCL and are liable for transfer to other subsidiaries of Coal India Limited.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for document verification or joining (except 2nd class Non-AC fare for SC/ST candidates for CBT as per rules).

Indian Navy (Indian Navy) Agniveer (MR/Musician) Notification 2026 has been released on 11 April 2026 on the official website https://www.joinindiannavy.gov.in/. The vacancies will be decided based on service requirement through the Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026.

Candidates born between 01 Jul 2004 to 31 Dec 2008 who have passed Matriculation (10th) with minimum 50% marks along with musical proficiency are eligible to apply for these Agniveer (Musician) vacancies. The online application process for Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 has started on 11 April 2026 and will remain open until 26 April 2026.

Candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply online through the direct links provided below in this article.

Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Online applications are invited for Agniveer (MR/Musician) - 02/2026 (Jul 26) batch on the official website joinindiannavy.gov.in. Candidates should thoroughly read the official advertisement to check eligibility criteria, age limit, musical standards, selection process and detailed instructions.

Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Educational Qualification: Candidate must have passed Matriculation Examination with minimum 50% marks in aggregate from the Boards of School Education recognised by Ministry of Education, Govt. of India.

Age Limit: Born between 01 Jul 2004 – 31 Dec 2008 (both dates inclusive).

Marital Status: Only unmarried male and unmarried female candidates are eligible. Candidates will have to give a certificate of being 'unmarried' at the time of enrolment. Agniveers shall not be permitted to marry during their entire tenure of four years. Marriage during tenure will result in dismissal.

Musical Ability: The candidate should have proficiency and aptitude for music with accuracy in tempo, pitch and singing one complete song. He/She should also have actual practical skill on any instrument of Indian or foreign origin with proficiency in tuning of instrument, matching unknown notes with instrument, prepared piece, sight reading of Indian Classical/Tablature/Staff Notation, performance of Scale/Raaga/Taala and basic theory of Indian or International music.

Type of Instruments on which Proficiency Required: Candidates would be required to perform with expertise on one or more instruments such as Keyboard/String/Wind instruments or any other instrument of Indian or foreign origin. Candidates performing on indefinite pitch instruments such as Jazz Drum set, Bass Drum, Snare Drums, Side Drums, Cymbals, Non Chromatic instruments such as bugle & monotone instruments used to produce only one note or drone will not be eligible.

Musical Experience Certificate:

  • Candidates performing Hindustani or Carnatic Classical music on wind instruments will require a musical experience certificate from any recognized musical institute or organisation of repute.
  • A certificate of minimum initial grade from international boards such as Trinity College of Music, London or Royal School of Music London etc., will be mandatory for candidates performing western notation or western music on wind instruments.
  • Other candidates will be required to submit certificates of participation/awards presented at various events.

Physical Standards:

  • Minimum Height: 157 cm (Male & Female)
  • Visual Standards: 6/12, 6/12 (Uncorrected) | 6/6, 6/6 (Corrected) | CP Pass

Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 Pay, Allowances & Benefits

Agniveers will be paid a package of ₹30,000 per month with a fixed yearly increment. In addition, risk and hardship, dress and travel allowances will be paid.

Seva Nidhi Package (One-time on completion of 4 years):

Year Customised Package (Monthly) In Hand (70%) Contribution to Agniveer Corpus Fund (30%) Contribution to Corpus Fund by GoI
1st Year 30,000 21,000 9,000 9,000
2nd Year 33,000 23,100 9,900 9,900
3rd Year 36,500 25,550 10,950 10,950
4th Year 40,000 28,000 12,000 12,000
Total - - 5.02 Lakh 5.02 Lakh
  • Life Insurance Cover: ₹48 Lakh (non-contributory)
  • Death Compensation: ₹44 Lakh (one-time ex-gratia) + Insurance
  • Disability Compensation: ₹44/25/15 Lakh (based on % disability)

Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

Selection of recruits is based on the order of merit based on their performance in Music Ability Test.

  1. Shortlisting: Based on marks obtained in Matriculation examination (All India merit). Only candidates holding valid music certificates will be considered.
  2. Recruitment Process: Physical Fitness Test (PFT) → Written Examination → Music Ability Test → Recruitment Medical Examination.
  3. Physical Fitness Test (PFT) – Qualifying:
Gender 1.6 Km Run Squats (Uthak Baithak) Push-ups Bent Sit-ups Knee Sit-ups
Male 06 min 30 sec 20 15 15 15
Female 08 min 15 10 10 10
  1. Written Examination: 50 MCQs (Science & Mathematics + General Awareness), 30 minutes, bilingual, minimum 25% marks required.
  2. Music Ability Test: Qualifying for final selection.
  3. Recruitment Medical Examination & Pre-Enrolment Medicals at INS Chilka.

Final Merit List: Based on performance in Music Ability Test (All India basis).

How to Apply for Indian Navy Agniveer (MR/Musician) Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must apply online through the official Indian Navy portal before the last date.

Step 1 – Visit www.joinindiannavy.gov.in and register.
Step 2 – Fill the online application form with correct details and upload scanned copies of all required documents (including music certificates, Matriculation marksheet, photograph with slate).
Step 3 – Verify and submit the application.
Step 4 – Download and print the application for future reference.

Important Note: Recent passport size colour photograph (taken not before Mar 2026) with name and date written on slate is mandatory. Applications through speed post/registered post will not be accepted.

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Eligibility Criteria

Educational Qualification: B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc. (Engineering) or 5-year Integrated M.Tech with a minimum of 60% aggregate marks in one of the 6 advertised disciplines from a university/institution recognised by AICTE/UGC. Only full-time, on-campus regular degrees are eligible — correspondence, distance education, part-time, and research-based degrees are not accepted.

GATE Score: Candidates must hold a valid GATE 2024, GATE 2025, or GATE 2026 score in the same discipline as their qualifying degree. GATE 2023 and earlier scores are not valid. Equal weightage is given to all three years' GATE scores.

Final Year Applicants: Candidates awaiting results or appearing in their final semester/year exam during 2026 may apply, but must submit their final result (minimum 60%) by 30 November 2026 if selected.

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Stipend & Pay Scale

During Training: Monthly Stipend — Rs. 74,000/- | One-time Book Allowance — Rs. 30,000/- | Mandatory lodging & boarding provided by NPCIL.

After Appointment as Scientific Officer/C (Group A): Pay Level 10 — Rs. 56,100/- in Pay Matrix + 58% DA (as on 01.07.2025) + Transport Allowance, HRA, Site Location Allowance, Professional Update Allowance, LTA, Medical (CHSS), Children Education Assistance, Performance Linked Incentive, EPF, Gratuity, Group Insurance, and NPCIL Defined Contribution Pension Scheme 2025.

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Application Fee

Category Fee
General / EWS / OBC (Male candidates only) Rs. 500/- (non-refundable)
Female candidates Nil (exempted)
SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-servicemen / DODPKIA / NPCIL employees Nil (exempted)

Fee is payable online only via debit card, credit card, net banking, or UPI through the integrated payment gateway. No other mode accepted.

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Selection Process

Candidates are shortlisted for Personal Interview based on GATE 2024/2025/2026 scores in a 1:12 ratio (discipline and category wise). GATE score carries no weightage in the final selection — final selection is purely based on Personal Interview performance and medical fitness.

Stage Details
Shortlisting Based on GATE 2024/2025/2026 score (1:12 ratio, merit list discipline & category wise)
Personal Interview Tentatively 8–19 June 2026 at Mumbai, NAPS (UP), MAPS (Tamil Nadu), KGS (Karnataka)
Qualifying marks (Interview) UR — 70% | EWS/SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD — 60%
Tie-breaking Higher GATE score → if still tied, senior in age ranks higher
Medical fitness Mandatory — by NPCIL's authorised medical officer
Bond Rs. 9,18,000/- indemnity bond + service bond for minimum 3 years after training

NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026 – Training Details

Selected candidates undergo a one-year orientation training at one of NPCIL's Nuclear Training Centres (NTC). Candidates in Mechanical, Chemical, Electrical, Electronics, and Instrumentation disciplines must choose a Reactor Stream — either Pressurised Heavy Water Reactor (PHWR) or Light Water Reactor (LWR). Civil discipline candidates need not indicate a reactor stream preference. After successful training completion, candidates are appointed as Scientific Officer/C in Group A on a one-year probation.

How to Apply for NPCIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026

1. Register: Visit www.npcilcareers.co.in and click Apply → Online Registration. Enter your GATE registration number (from GATE 2024/2025/2026 admit card), discipline, personal details, and category. An activation link will be sent to your registered email.

2. Activate your account: Click the activation link in your email to activate your login.

3. Fill application form: Log in and complete all stages — educational qualifications, post-qualification work experience (if any), personal details, interview city preference, reactor stream choice (if applicable), and upload scanned photo (JPEG, max 50 KB) and signature (JPEG, max 20 KB).

4. Submit application: Review the draft carefully and click "Submit Application." No changes can be made after submission.

5. Pay application fee (if applicable): General/EWS/OBC male candidates pay Rs. 500/- online via debit/credit card, net banking, or UPI. Fee payment window: 10 April – 30 April 2026. Note your application number on successful payment.

The Online application process for Central Bank of India Recruitment 2026 has started on 09-04-2026 and will remain open until 30-04-2026. The candidate shall apply online through Central Bank of India website, centralbank.bank.in.

Central Bank of India Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF Download

The Central Bank of India Specialist Officers Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF has been released on 08-04-2026 at centralbank.bank.in. Check the complete job details, vacancy, age limit, application fee, selection process and how to apply from the article. You can Check all Latest  Updates of All Central Government Jobs and State Government Jobs.

Central Bank of India Specialist Officers Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Name of the Post: Central Bank of India Specialist Officers Online Form 2026

Post Date: 08-04-2026

Total Vacancy: 26 Posts

Brief Information: The Central Bank of India has announced a notification for the recruitment of Specialist Officers Vacancy. Eligible Candidates who are interested in the vacancy details & completed all eligibility criteria can read the notification & Apply.

Central Bank of India Recruitment 2026 Notification Overview

The Central Bank of India has officially released a recruitment notification for Specialist Officers. For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

Central Bank of India

Specialist Officers Vacancy 2026

WWW.OnlyJobs

Application Fee

  • SC/ST/PwBD/Women: ₹175/- (Intimation Charges only)
  • All Other Candidates: ₹850/- (Application Fee + Intimation Charges)

Central Bank of India Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

  • Starting Date for Apply Online: 09-04-2026
  • Last Date for Apply Online: 30-04-2026
  • Tentative Date of Online Examination: May 2026
  • Tentative Date of Interview: June 2026

Central Bank of India Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

  • Age limit Scale IV: 20-45 Years
  • Age limit Scale III / II: 20-35 Years
  • Age relaxation is applicable as per rules.

Qualification

  • Analytics & Model Risk (Scale IV/III): Bachelor’s in Mathematics/Statistics or BE/B.Tech in Data Science/IT/Computer Science/Software Engineering + 5/3 years post-qualification experience in quantitative/risk analytics/model validation (Banks/NBFC/Investment Banks). Preferred: MBA/PGDM in Finance, FRM/CFA/CQF certifications.
  • Asset Liability Management (Scale III/II): BE/B.Tech in IT/CS/Software Engg. or MBA/CA + 4/3 years total experience (at least 3/2 years in Risk Management in Banks/NBFCs).
  • Digital Risk Management (Scale III/II): Bachelor’s in Computer Science/IT/Cybersecurity + 4/3 years total experience (at least 3/2 years in Risk Management). Preferred: CISA/CISM/CRISC/CISSP.
  • Enterprise Risk (Scale III): BE/B.Tech in IT/CS/Software Engg. or MBA/CA + 5 years total experience (at least 3 years in Risk Management).
  • Market Risk (Scale III/II): BE/B.Tech in IT/CS/Software Engg. or MBA/CA + 4/3 years total experience (at least 3/2 years in Risk Management).

Remuneration (Scale of Pay)

  • Scale IV (Chief Manager): ₹102300-2980/4-114220-3360/2-120940
  • Scale III (Senior Manager): ₹85920-2680/5-99320-2980/2-105280
  • Scale II (Manager): ₹64820-2340/1-67160-2680/10-93960
  • (Perquisites/allowances as per Bank’s policy)

Selection Process

  • The selection process for Central Bank of India Specialist Officers in Risk Management streams consists of an online written examination followed by a personal interview.

  • The final merit will be prepared by giving 70% weightage to the online written test and 30% weightage to the interview, subject to document verification and medical fitness.

  • Only those candidates who qualify the online exam and meet the bank’s cutoff will be called for the interview in a prescribed ratio to the number of vacancies.

How to Apply

Visit official website

  • Go to the official Central Bank of India website: centralbank.bank.in.

  • Open the “Recruitment/ Careers” section and click on the link for “Recruitment Process for Selection of Officers in Specialist Category in Risk Management Streams.”

Open Apply Online link

  • On the recruitment page, click on “Click Here to Apply” / “Apply Online” which redirects to the online registration page (IBPS application link for CBI SO).

  • Read the detailed notification, “How to Apply” and FAQ on the registration page carefully before proceeding.

New registration

  • Click on “New Registration” and enter basic details (name, contact number, email ID, etc.) to generate a Registration Number and Password.

  • Note down the Registration Number and Password for future login and form completion.

Fill online application form

  • Log in using Registration Number and Password and select the relevant post/scale (Analytics & Model Risk, Asset Liability Management, Digital Risk Management, Enterprise Risk, Market Risk in Scale IV/III/II as applicable).

  • Fill personal details, category, domicile, address, qualification (B.Sc/B.Tech/BE/MBA/PGDM etc.) and post-qualification experience in Risk/IT/ALM/Digital Risk as per eligibility.

  • Ensure all experience details (years, organisation, role) match what is required for the chosen scale and stream.

Upload required documents

  • Upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, left thumb impression and handwritten declaration in the prescribed dimensions and file size, as per the instructions in the notification.

  • Upload supporting documents (if asked at application stage) like educational certificates, experience certificates and category certificates in the specified format.

Pay application fee

  • Pay the application fee online: ₹175/- (intimation charges) for SC/ST/PwBD/Women candidates and ₹850/- (application fee + intimation charges) for all other candidates, through debit card/credit card/net banking/UPI.

  • After successful payment, verify that the fee status shows as “paid” and proceed to final submit. Applications without successful fee payment (where applicable) will be treated as incomplete.

Final submission and print

  • Review all details carefully; edit if required before final submission, as no changes are allowed after final submit.

  • Click on “Final Submit” and generate your application form.

  • Download and take a printout of the e-receipt and submitted application form for future reference, online exam and interview/document verification stages.

General Instructions for Candidates

  • Apply online only; no other mode of application will be accepted.

  • Ensure you fall within the required age limits for Scale IV (20–45 years) and Scale III/II (20–35 years) with applicable relaxations as per Government/Bank rules.

  • Enter details exactly as per your certificates; any mismatch or false information may lead to rejection at any stage of the recruitment process.

  • Keep the registered mobile number and email ID active; all communication regarding exam date, admit card, interview schedule, etc., will be sent on these only.

  • Candidates are advised to complete the application process well before 30-04-2026 to avoid last-minute technical issues.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Electronics Corporation of India Limited (ECIL), a Miniratna Category‑I PSU under the Department of Atomic Energy, has released ECIL Recruitment 2026 Notification Advt. No. 07/2026 for walk‑in selections to various fixed tenure contract posts in its business verticals across the country.

The notification invites dynamic and experienced candidates for Project Engineer on Contract (PE‑C), Technical Officer on Contract (TO‑C), Assistant Project Engineer on Contract (APE‑C) and Senior Artisan on Contract (SA‑C) with postings in North, West and South Zones including New Delhi, Chandigarh, Ferozepur, Pathankot, Rawatbhata, Mumbai, Kakrapar, Bengaluru, Kaiga, Kudankulam, Kalpakkam and Chennai.

Engagement will be purely on contract basis for an initial period of one year, extendable up to four years (two years for one Kudankulam PE‑C category) based on project requirements and performance, with consolidated monthly emoluments up to Rs. 55,000 for Project Engineers and other benefits like medical insurance reimbursement, PF and TA/DA as per rules.

ECIL Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Notification PDF for ECIL Contract Recruitment 2026 is available on the ECIL careers page under the title “Walk‑in Selections for various Contract Posts (Advt. No. 07/2026)”. Candidates should download and read the ECIL notification carefully to check detailed post‑wise qualifications, experience, age limits, reservation, salary, selection criteria and complete instructions for appearing in the walk‑in.

Document Download Link
ECIL Contract Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF (Advt. 07/2026) Download Notification PDF
ECIL Contract Application Form 2026 (PE‑C / TO‑C / APE‑C / SA‑C)

ECIL Recruitment 2026 - Overview

Particulars Details
Organization Electronics Corporation of India Limited (ECIL), a Govt. of India (DAE) enterprise, Miniratna Category‑I 
Advertisement Number & Date Advt. No. 07/2026, dated 26.03.2026 
Post Names (on contract) Project Engineer, Technical Officer, Assistant Project Engineer, Senior Artisan
Tenure Initial 1 year, extendable up to 4 years (including initial term) depending on project requirement & performance; one PE‑C post at Kudankulam extendable up to 2 years 
Work Locations/Zones New Delhi & Chandigarh & Ferozepur & Pathankot & Rawatbhata (North Zone); Mumbai & Kakrapar (West Zone); Bengaluru & Kaiga (South Zone); Kudankulam, Kalpakkam & Chennai (South Zone) 
Qualification B.E./B.Tech., Diploma, ITI
Upper Age Limit (UR) 30 and 33 Years
Selection Method Walk‑in registration & document verification,
Application Mode Walk‑in only – candidates must lication form from www.ecil.co.in, fill and carry with original & self‑attested documents to venue; no online form submission 
Official Website https://www.ecil.co.in (Careers/Recruitment section, Advt. No. 07/2026) 

ECIL Recruitment 2026 Vacancy 2026 Details

A total of 27 vacancies will be filled for Project Engineer, Technical Officer, Assistant Project Engineer and Senior Artisan posts under the ECIL Recruitment 2026 (Advt. No. 07/2026) on fixed tenure contract basis across North, West and South Zones. Check ECIL post wise contract vacancy details in the table below.

Post ECIL Vacancy 2026
Project Engineer on Contract (PE‑C) 14
Technical Officer on Contract (TO‑C) 08
Assistant Project Engineer on Contract (APE‑C) 03
Senior Artisan on Contract (SA‑C) 07
Total ECIL Contract Posts 32

ECIL Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates (walk‑in schedule)

ECIL Contract Recruitment 2026 Events Important Dates & Details
Date of ECIL Advt. 07/2026 notification 26 March 2026 
Walk‑in date – Mumbai (West Zone) 09 April 2026 – ECIL Zonal Office, #1207, Veer Savarkar Marg, Dadar (Prabhadevi), Mumbai – 400028 
Walk‑in date – New Delhi (North Zone) 09 April 2026 – ECIL Zonal Office, D‑15, DDA Local Shopping Complex, A‑Block, Ring Road, Naraina, New Delhi – 110028 
Walk‑in date – Bengaluru (South Zone) 20 April 2026 – ECIL Zonal Office, #1/1, 2nd Floor, LIC Building, Sampige Road, Malleswaram, Bengaluru – 560003 
Walk‑in date – Chennai (South Zone) 20 April 2026 – ECIL Zonal Office, Economist House, S‑15, Industrial Estate, Guindy, Chennai – 600032 
Walk‑in registration time at venues 09:00 hrs to 11:30 hrs; candidates reporting after 11:30 hrs will not be allowed to register 
Cut‑off date for age & post‑qualification experience Date of selection (respective walk‑in date) 

ECIL Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria 2026

  • Project Engineer on Contract (PE‑C): B.E./B.Tech with at least 60% aggregate marks (50% for SC/ST where reserved) in relevant disciplines such as Electronics, ECE, ETC, EEE, E&I, CSE, IT, CSIT, Mechanical/Electrical with NDT Level‑II (for Kudankulam) along with a minimum of 3 years post‑qualification experience; apprenticeship in the relevant field up to 1 year is also counted as experience.
  • Technical Officer on Contract (TO‑C): B.E./B.Tech with at least 60% aggregate marks (50% for SC/ST where reserved) in Electronics/Electrical/ECE/ETC/EEE/E&I/CSE/IT and minimum 1 year post‑qualification experience; apprenticeship up to 1 year is countable towards experience.
  • Assistant Project Engineer on Contract (APE‑C): Diploma with at least 60% aggregate marks (50% for SC/ST where reserved) in ECE/ETC/Electronics/EEE/Instrumentation and minimum 1 year post‑qualification experience including relevant apprenticeship.
  • Senior Artisan on Contract (SA‑C): ITI passed in Electronics, Electrician, Electronic Mechanic, Instrumentation or Radio & TV trades with minimum 2 years post‑qualification experience (including up to 1 year apprenticeship) in maintenance/repair of electronics, instrumentation systems, communication gadgets or computers.
  • Age Limit (UR): Project Engineer – maximum 33 years; Technical Officer, Assistant Project Engineer and Senior Artisan – maximum 30 years. Age relaxation is 3 years for OBC, 5 years for SC/ST, an additional 10 years for PwBD (40% and above) over category relaxation, and 5 years for candidates domiciled in J&K from 01.01.1980 to 31.12.1989.

ECIL Recruitment 2026 Salary 2026

  • Selected candidates under ECIL Contract Recruitment 2026 will receive consolidated monthly emoluments based on the post and contract year, along with limited benefits as per ECIL rules.
  • Consolidated Emoluments: Project Engineer on Contract – Rs. 40,000 (1st year), Rs. 45,000 (2nd year), Rs. 50,000 (3rd year), Rs. 55,000 (4th year) plus a one‑time attire allowance of Rs. 8,400 after completion of at least six months; Technical Officer on Contract – Rs. 25,000 (1st year), Rs. 28,000 (2nd year), Rs. 31,000 (3rd & 4th years); Assistant Project Engineer – Rs. 25,506 per month; Senior Artisan – Rs. 23,368 per month, with APE‑C and SA‑C wages revised every six months as per CLC (Central) guidelines.

ECIL Recruitment 2026 Application Fees

  • The ECIL Advt. No. 07/2026 walk‑in notification does not prescribe any separate application fee for appearing in the selection process for these contract posts.
  • Fee Status: Candidates are required to download the application form and attend the walk‑in with documents; there is no mention of any application or examination fee in the advertisement, so applicants should not pay any money to unauthorized persons or intermediaries and rely only on official ECIL instructions.

ECIL Recruitment 2026 Application Form

There is no online application process for ECIL Recruitment 2026 (Advt. No. 07/2026); candidates have to attend walk‑in selections as per the notified schedule. Eligible applicants must visit the official ECIL website ecil.co.in, download the prescribed ECIL contract application form 2026, fill it carefully and report at the concerned zonal walk‑in venue on the respective date within the registration time slot.

Candidates must carry the duly filled ECIL application form, updated resume, original certificates and one set of self‑attested photocopies for age, qualification, experience, category, disability, ex‑servicemen and identity proof along with recent passport size photographs. Registration and document verification will be done at the venue before the personal interview under ECIL Walk‑in Recruitment 2026.

Click to Download ECIL Contract Application Form 2026

Download ECIL Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF (Advt. No. 07/2026)

ECIL Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

  • The ECIL Contract Recruitment 2026 selection for Project Engineer, Technical Officer, Assistant Project Engineer and Senior Artisan will be based on walk‑in registration, document verification and personal interview with a weighted merit system.
  • Shortlisting & Evaluation: After on‑spot registration and successful verification of original documents, candidates will be evaluated on three components – (A) Qualification: 20% of aggregate percentage scored in BE/B.Tech/Diploma/ITI; (B) Relevant Experience: up to 30 marks based on post‑qualification experience as per post‑specific rules; and (C) Personal Interview: 50 marks.
  • Final Merit & Offer: Final recommendations will be prepared in order of merit based on total score (A+B+C). Experience from purely academic internships, college projects etc. is not counted. Selected candidates will be engaged on fixed‑tenure contract and must be willing to work anywhere in India as per ECIL’s project requirements.

How to Apply / Attend ECIL Walk‑in Recruitment 2026

Candidates meeting the eligibility criteria for ECIL Contract Recruitment 2026 (Advt. 07/2026) must appear personally at the designated zonal venues on the scheduled walk‑in dates with the prescribed application form and documents.

  • Step 1 – Download ECIL Application Form: Visit the official ECIL website www.ecil.co.in, open the Careers/Recruitment section and download the application form for Advt. No. 07/2026. Take a printout and fill it neatly with all required details including post category, zone and location preference.
  • Step 2 – Prepare Documents: Keep ready original and one set of self‑attested copies of 10th certificate (DOB proof), all qualification certificates and marksheets, CGPA‑to‑percentage conversion (if applicable), complete experience certificates with from‑to dates and post held, appointment order and recent payslips (if currently employed), caste (EWS/OBC/SC/ST) certificate, non‑creamy layer OBC certificate (not older than one year), PwBD certificate, Ex‑servicemen/ J&K domicile proof and valid government ID (Aadhaar/Passport etc.).
  • Step 3 – Reach Walk‑in Venue in Time: Report to the respective ECIL Zonal Office (Mumbai, New Delhi, Bengaluru on 20 April 2026; Chennai on 20 April 2026) within the registration time 09:00 hrs to 11:30 hrs. Late reporting after 11:30 hrs will not be accepted under any circumstances.
  • Step 4 – Registration & Document Verification: Submit the filled ECIL application form 2026, resume and document set at the registration desk. After successful verification, candidates will be allowed to attend the personal interview as part of the selection process.
  • Step 5 – Attend Interview & Wait for Result: Appear for the interview as scheduled at the venue. Final selection will be based on the combined weightage of qualification, experience and interview performance. Shortlisted candidates will receive joining instructions from ECIL and must be prepared for posting at project locations anywhere in India.
