How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About this page: Sarkari Job 2026 information on this page is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team from official government recruitment notifications. All vacancy data is verified against official websites before listing. OnlyJobs has been India's trusted government job portal since 2011, serving 10+ million users annually. For any recruitment, always refer to the official notification linked above.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs
  • State Government Jobs: State PSCs, Police, Teachers, Healthcare workers

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 775+ active job listings.

Candidates can check state-wise and district-wise Anganwadi vacancy lists, eligibility, qualifications, age limit, salary, and online application dates, and download the Anganwadi Application Form 2026. Anganwadi Worker Salary / Honorarium 2026

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

About this page: Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 information is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team directly from official state WCD / ICDS recruitment notifications. We verify all vacancy details, eligibility, and last dates before publishing. Page is updated daily as new notifications are released. For any discrepancy, always refer to the official notification PDF linked in the apply column above.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)

  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Post Pay Level Basic Pay Gross Salary (Approx) Category
Forest Guard / Van Rakshak / Vana Palaka Level 3 ₹21,700 ₹28,000 – ₹35,000 Group C
Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C
Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Ranger Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹70,000 – ₹90,000 Group B
Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF) Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹75,000 – ₹95,000 Group B
Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) Level 12 ₹78,800 ₹1,00,000 – ₹1,20,000 Group A
Conservator of Forests (CF) Level 13 ₹1,23,100 ₹1,50,000 – ₹1,80,000 Group A
Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF) Level 14 ₹1,44,200 ₹1,80,000 – ₹2,10,000 IFS Cadre
Additional PCCF (Addl. PCCF) Level 15 ₹1,82,200 ₹2,10,000 – ₹2,20,000 IFS Cadre
Principal Chief Conservator (PCCF / HoFF) Level 17 ₹2,25,000 ₹2,25,000 (fixed) Apex Scale

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

Indian Bank Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates holding qualifications such as B.Tech/B.E, CA, MBA/PGDM, MCA and other prescribed degrees with the required experience can apply online for these Specialist positions. The online application process started on April 8, 2026 and will remain open until April 28, 2026. Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official website, indianbank.bank.in, via the “Recruitment of Specialist Officers – 2026” link or directly through the IBPS registration portal at ibpsreg.ibps.in/ibsofeb26/.

Selection Process

Selection will be made through shortlisting of applications followed by Interview (or Online Examination + Interview, if conducted by the Bank).

  • Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria will be shortlisted for Interview (or Online Test + Interview) based on the information furnished in the online application and supporting documents.
  • The Bank reserves the right to conduct Online Examination / Group Discussion / Interview or any combination thereof as per its requirement.
  • Final selection will be based on the candidate’s performance in the selection process, subject to verification of documents and medical fitness.
  • The Bank may shortlist candidates in the ratio decided by the Bank and call them for Interview.
  • Merit list will be prepared post-Interview and provisional offer of appointment will be issued to the selected candidates.
  • The decision of the Bank in all matters relating to selection shall be final and binding.

How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through the official website of Indian Bank. No other mode of application will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website of Indian Bank and go to the “Recruitment” / “Careers” section.
  2. Click on the link for “Recruitment of Specialist Officers - 2026” and register using a valid email ID and mobile number.
  3. Fill the online application form carefully with all mandatory details (personal information, educational qualification, experience, category etc.).
  4. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and all required documents/certificates (educational, experience, category, PwBD etc.) as per specifications.
  5. Pay the requisite application fee / intimation charges online.
  6. Submit the application and take a printout of the final submitted application form for future reference.
  7. A candidate can apply for only one post. In case of multiple applications, only the latest valid (completed) application will be retained.

General Instructions

  • Cut-off date for determining eligibility (age, qualification, experience, etc.) is 01-04-2026.
  • Only one application should be submitted by any candidate. Multiple applications will lead to cancellation of candidature.
  • Candidates must ensure that they fulfil all the eligibility criteria mentioned in the advertisement. Mere submission of application does not guarantee eligibility.
  • The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank.
  • Reservation for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD will be provided as per Government of India guidelines. PwBD reservation is on horizontal basis.
  • Candidates belonging to reserved categories must upload valid category certificates issued on or before the last date of registration.
  • All educational qualifications and experience must be from Government recognized Universities / Institutions and should be active as on the date of application.
  • The Bank reserves the right to cancel / modify the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification of the candidature.
  • Decision of the Bank in all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications, mode of selection, etc. shall be final and binding on the candidates.
  • All future communications (call letters, results, etc.) will be sent only through the registered email ID and SMS. Candidates are advised to keep the same email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process.
  • Any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Courts in Chennai only

Selection Process

The direct recruitment will be done through a three-stage process:

  • Stage 1: Screening Test (qualifying in nature)
    • Online MCQ (150 questions, 3 hours duration) on Reasoning, Basic Mathematics, English, Aptitude, Attitude and Leadership Quality.
    • Medium: English only.
    • Qualifying marks: Minimum 60% (relaxable to 50% for OBC and 40% for SC/ST/PwBD if sufficient candidates not available).
    • Shortlisting ratio: Restricted to 1:6 (six candidates per post) in order of merit in each category.
  • Stage 2: Evaluation of Academic Records
    • Applications qualifying the Screening Test will be evaluated by a Screening Evaluation Committee.
    • Marks awarded as per detailed norms given in Annexure-I (Scientist-C) and Annexure-II (Scientist-D) (total 120 marks).
    • Shortlisting ratio for Interview: 1:3 (three candidates per post) in order of merit.
  • Stage 3: Personal Interaction / Interview
    • 30 marks.
    • Conducted at Delhi or any other location/mode approved by competent authority.
    • Final Merit List: Marks of Evaluation (120) + Interview (30) = 150 marks.

Document Verification: Original documents will be verified prior to the interview. Candidates failing to produce all required original documents will be debarred.

Note: Screening Test is qualifying only. Final selection is based on combined marks of Evaluation + Interview. Normalization of marks will be applied if the test is conducted in multiple batches.

How to Apply

Candidates must apply ONLINE only at https://recruitment.nic.in/ between 08-04-2026 to 30-04-2026 (05:30 PM). No other mode of application will be accepted.

Four Mandatory Steps:

  1. Registration
  2. Fill Online Application Form & Upload Documents (Photo, Signature, Age Proof, Qualification Certificates, Experience Certificates, Caste/PwBD Certificates, NOC/CCA Declaration, SOP, etc. – full checklist given in the advertisement)
  3. Payment of Application Fee (if applicable)
  4. Download Confirmation Page

Important:

  • Application is complete only after all four steps.
  • Aadhaar-based authentication will be used at every stage.
  • Candidates can apply for more than one post but must pay separate fee for each.
  • Change of category is not permitted after submission.
  • Serving Government employees must upload signed CCA Declaration & Acknowledgement (Annexure-VI).

Application Fee (non-refundable):

  • Nil – SC/ST/PwBD/Women candidates
  • Rs. 800/- per post – General and all others

General Instructions

  • The number of posts is tentative and may increase or decrease.
  • Place of posting: Anywhere in India and outside India.
  • Cut-off date for age, qualification and experience: 21-11-2022.
  • All communications will be sent only through registered email ID and mobile number. Candidates must regularly check their email/SMS and the official recruitment portal.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for attending the Screening Test or Interview.
  • Mere fulfilling of minimum eligibility does not entitle a candidate to be called for the next stage.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • Any false information or forged documents will result in cancellation of candidature and legal action.
  • Decision of NIC/MeitY in all matters relating to recruitment shall be final.
  • Jurisdiction: Any dispute will be subject to the jurisdiction of courts in Delhi only.

Selection Process

Selection will be made through Computer Based Test (CBT) only.

  • The CBT will comprise of objective type questions with multiple choice of answers. The Question Paper and instructions will be Bilingual (English/Hindi). However, in case of any error in Hindi version, the English version of the question shall be valid & final.
  • The CBT will consist of two sections (Section – ‘A’ & Section – ‘B’):
    • Section – ‘A’: Questions related to Technical Knowledge (Discipline related) with 80 Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ).
    • Section – ‘B’: Questions related to General Awareness/Aptitude and Knowledge of CIL/SECL with 20 multiple choice questions (MCQ).
  • Each question will carry 1 mark and there is no penalty for wrong answer. No marks will be awarded for un-attempted questions.
  • Syllabus for the Computer Based Test (CBT) is attached as Annexure-A in the notification.
  • Selection of candidates in merit panel will be subject to securing of cut-off marks (Minimum Qualifying marks) in the Computer Based Test for concerned post with respect to their category as mentioned below and fulfilling prescribed eligibility criteria:
Category Qualifying marks in CBT
For General/EWS Candidates 45 marks
For OBC(NCL)/ESM Candidates 40 marks
For SC/ST Candidates 35 marks
  • Result of the Computer Based Test (CBT) will be published on SECL website as early as possible after successful completion of the CBT.
  • Provisional merit panels of candidates who secure the requisite cut-off marks and above and fulfilling the eligibility criteria will be published, in the order of marks scored, after resolving tie.
  • In case of tie of marks between 02 or more candidates, the merit-list will be prepared as per the following criteria: a) Date of Birth (As per the descending order of age of candidates) b) Seniority as per the Date of issue of Statutory Certificate c) In case still tied up, alphabetical order of the name of the candidates as recorded in the application form may be taken into consideration. d) In case still tied-up, the Selection Committee will decide the criteria for deciding the merit position of tied candidates.
  • Final merit panel will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the Computer Based Test.
  • Such candidates who would score equal or above the cut off (Minimum qualifying marks as provided in Table D) and as per vacancy available in each category, would only be notified for appearing for scrutiny/ verification of their testimonials supporting their claim of candidature, in order of their respective merit position drawn on the basis of marks scored in Computer Based Test (CBT) for different positions up to the extent the positions remain unfilled. The candidates shall have to appear along with all original certificates/ documents as per eligibility criteria notified in this Employment Notification for scrutiny/ verification. The date, time and venue for scrutiny/ verification shall be notified on SECL website and the candidates shall have to report accordingly. In the event of failure to report with all original certificates and testimonials within the stipulated time, the candidature of such candidate should stand cancelled.
  • Provisional Appointment Letter will be issued to the successful candidates in accordance with their relative merit position and with due consideration of the reservation of posts to the reserved category candidates and against vacancies.
  • If it is found during the Scrutiny/ Verification, or during the course of training, or even during the course of employment, that the candidate is not fulfilling the eligibility criteria in any respect, suppressing of facts or false declaration pertaining to his Employment/Caste/ Testimonials etc. then his/her candidature/ employment will be cancelled/terminated.

Note: In case, a candidate, selected for a particular post & declared as UNFIT during Initial Medical Examination, the case would be referred to Apex Medical Board of the Company within one month based on the written/physical representation and the decision of the Apex Medical Board will be final. If the Candidate is declared UNFIT by the Apex Medical Board, his/ her selection shall stand automatically cancelled. Such candidates will be communicated the decision of the Apex Medical Board as well as regarding cancellation of their selection.

2. How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through SECL website. No other mode (post, email, offline) will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website of SECL https://secl-cil.in/ → Career → Employment Notification for Direct Recruitment of various Statutory posts → Apply online.
  2. Employment Notification is available in both English and Hindi. Candidates may select either of them at their convenience.
  3. While applying online, candidate needs to upload the scanned copies of:
    • Recent passport size colour photograph (not older than 3 weeks in jpg/jpeg format)
    • Signature with Black ink pen (in jpg/jpeg format)
    • Matriculation certificate or equivalent examination certificate or Degree or Diploma Certificate for the post of Mining Sirdar & Dy. Surveyor (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Matriculate or equivalent examination passed from any recognized Board of examination & Diploma or relevant and higher qualification (Minimum 3 years course) in Electrical Engineering from any Institute recognized by the Government, for the post of Assistant Foreman (Electrical) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Valid Caste Certificate OBC (NCL)/SC/ST candidates and Income Certificate for EWS Candidates (if applicable) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Ex-Servicemen Certificates (if applicable) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • PwBD Certificates (if applicable) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Competency certificate of Mining Sirdar/Surveyor (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Gas Testing & First Aid Certificate (for Mining Sirdar only) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
  4. Register by filling up necessary details (Post opted, name, Mobile No. and e-mail ID and click on Submit Button).
  5. Check the User ID and password received on your e-mail and mobile number after successful registration.
  6. Re-login to your account entering the User ID and Password received on Mobile No. and e-mail ID.
  7. Click on the option to go to application form and fill all the necessary details in the respective fields and upload all the required documents including Photograph and Signature.
  8. Check Preview of the Application Form and make corrections, if any.
  9. Press Submit Button and make Online Payment of the fees through Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking (as applicable).
  10. Keep the print out of the online application form filled for future references.

Application Fee:

Category Amount (₹)
Unreserved (UR) /OBC- (Creamy Layer & Non-Creamy Layer) / EWS ₹1000/- + GST ₹180/- = ₹1180/-
SC/ ST/ESM/PwBD/ Departmental Candidates/Female Candidates Nil

Note: Applications without the prescribed fee will not be entertained and summarily rejected. No representation against such rejection would be entertained. Fee once paid shall neither be refunded nor be adjusted under any circumstances. Any tax or Bank charges other than the application fees will be borne by the candidates. Application fee will be paid through online mode only.

General Instructions

  • Application should be submitted through ONLINE Mode only.
  • All qualifications should be recognized by AICTE / UGC / appropriate Indian Statutory Authorities.
  • Before submitting the application, the candidate must ensure that he/ she fulfills all the eligibility criteria and other norms mentioned in the Employment Notification. He/ she may cross check the information such as Date of Birth, Category, Contact Details, and Qualifications etc. furnished in the application form before finally submitting the same online as no correction would be possible later and no correspondence will be considered in this regard.
  • The decision of SECL management regarding eligibility criteria, acceptance or rejection of applications, penalty for false information, mode of selection, selection and posting of selected candidates, will be final and binding on the candidates and no enquiry/correspondence will be entertained in this regard.
  • Mere issuance of Admit Card/ Call Letter to a candidate will not imply that his/ her candidature has been finally cleared by SECL. The candidature shall remain provisional till such time SECL verifies the eligibility conditions with reference to original documents after the candidate qualifies in the CBT and confirms appointment.
  • Candidates should ensure that they fulfill the eligibility criteria prescribed for the post for which they have applied. In case it is found at any stage of the selection process or even after the appointment that the candidate has submitted false or incorrect information or suppressed any relevant information/ material facts or does not fulfill the eligibility criteria, his/her candidature/ service is liable for rejection/ termination without notice.
  • No relaxation in the minimum educational qualification, as prescribed in Clause - '2' of this Employment Notification, is admissible for any category of candidate.
  • Wherever Grade (CGPA/ OGP/ DGPA etc.) is awarded, a certificate duly issued by concerned Board/University for conversion of Grade to percentage is to be produced by the candidate at the time of Scrutiny/ Verification of Original documents.
  • Age and all other eligibility criteria shall be reckoned as on the cut-off date as prescribed under this Employment Notification.
  • Candidates should carefully fill up their category (General/ SC/ ST/ OBC- Non Creamy Layer/ Ex-Servicemen/ EWS/PwBD etc.) in the Online Application Form. No change of category shall be allowed at the later stage of selection process.
  • Ex-servicemen must produce discharge certificate issued by Army/Navy/ Air Force of Indian Union at the time of document verification.
  • All documents (Caste Certificate, Educational Qualifications, EWS certificate, etc.) as per applicability and as provided by the candidate will be verified from the office of issuing authority and candidature will be cancelled if the certificates are not found satisfactory/ genuine at any subsequent stage of Computer Based Test (CBT).
  • The candidates must have an active E-mail Id & Mobile number which must remain valid for at least next one year. All correspondences with the candidates shall be done either through Speed Post/ Registered e-mail id & Mobile number (as may be necessary) and all general information shall be provided through SECL website. However, admit card will be provided only through SECL website and the candidates will have to download the admit card. The responsibilities of receiving, downloading and printing of application form, admit card and other information shall be of candidates. SECL will not be responsible for any loss of document due to invalid/ wrong/ incomplete contact details provided by the candidate or for delay/ non-receipt of information. In case of any problems faced by the candidates in filling up the online application they may refer the "Help Desk Option" available in the individual login portal. For queries other than the Online Application Form, candidates may write to Email Id: [email protected] only or can call us on 9425598240.
  • Candidates are advised to apply much before the closing date for submission of Online Application mentioned in this Employment Notification to avoid last minute network congestion.
  • SECL will not take any responsibility for the candidates not being able to submit their applications Online within the prescribed closing dates on account of any reasons beyond the control of SECL.
  • SECL reserves the right to shortlist candidates for Computer Based Test, reschedule the test date & venue etc. or alter any of the advertised condition depending upon the circumstances.
  • SECL will not bear any liability on account of service bond/ salary/ leave salary/ pension contribution, etc, if any, of previous employment of any candidate already working in Central Govt./ State Govt./ Autonomous Body/ Public Sector Undertaking.
  • Candidates employed in Govt./ Semi Govt./ Public Sector Undertaking (including departmental candidates) / Autonomous Bodies are required to produce 'No Objection Certificate' at the time of Scrutiny /Verification of original documents if shortlisted.
  • Selected candidates could be posted in any of the Mines/Establishments of SECL and are liable to be transferred to other subsidiaries of Coal India Limited any time during their service as may be required.
  • Appointment of selected candidates will be subject to being found medically fit in the Company's Medical Examination as per the laid down Rules related to Medical Examination of CIL.
  • Selection of candidate shall be provisional, subject to verification of documents relating to eligibility criteria, character, antecedents and other documents submitted by the candidate and is also subject to his/ her meeting other requirements applicable for appointment under the rules of SECL.
  • Appointment of the candidate in SECL will be subject to verification of Character & Antecedents by the prescribed authorities and if any discrepancy / suppression is found, the candidature of the candidate will be cancelled and decision of SECL in this regard shall be final.
  • The date of Computer Based Test (CBT) and other details will be subsequently informed. Candidates will have to appear at the Centre/ Venue allotted to them by SECL for the Computer Based Test (CBT). No change in Centre/ Venue is permissible and no application shall be entertained in this regard.
  • SC/ ST candidates called for Computer Based Test (CBT) will be paid up to IInd Class Non-AC fare as per rules. However they have to submit their claims in duly filled appropriate TA Claim format provided at the examination centre along with all desired enclosures like caste certificate, photocopy of Onward (mandatory) and return Journey (optional) of railway ticket / bus receipt ticket to the room invigilator after attempting Computer Based Test (CBT) only, subsequent claims as well as claims with incomplete information will not be entertained. The distance of examination centre will be calculated from the Permanent address of the candidate or from place of actual journey whichever is shorter. In case, candidate has submitted only onward journey ticket and not submitted return journey tickets then the claims will be considered up to the double of onward journey fare and that will be paid as per rule.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in the CBT, scrutiny/ verification of certificates and documents or for joining on being selected except otherwise as provided in this Employment Notification.
  • Candidate will appear for the CBT at the exam center at his/her owns risk and South Eastern Coalfields Limited will not be responsible for any injury or losses etc.
  • SECL management reserves the right to modify/ alter/ restrict/ enlarge/ cancel the recruitment process, and revise the number of vacancies, at its discretion without assigning any reasons whatsoever. The decision of the SECL management will be final and no appeal will be entertained in this regard.
  • All future communication/ information regarding this recruitment will be made available on SECL website. Candidates are advised to check/ visit SECL website regularly for further updates.
  • Court of Jurisdiction for any dispute will be Honorable High Court of Chhattisgarh, Bilaspur.
  • In case of any ambiguity/ confusion, English Version of Employment Notification will be considered valid.
  • Canvassing in any form will be treated as dis-qualification.
  • At the time of Computer Based Test (CBT), if a candidate is found guilty of using unfair means or impersonating or misbehaving in the examination hall or resorting to any other unfair means in connection with his/her candidature for the selection; or obtaining support of his/ her candidature by any means, such candidates will be liable to criminal prosecution and will be disqualified from the Computer Based Test (CBT). The Company may also debar such candidates from participating in any selection process in the Company in future.
  • If it is detected that any candidate has furnished any false information / particulars with regard to his/ her qualification, Category/caste and experience in the Application Form or in any document and/ or misrepresented / suppressed any material fact in the application form, for securing appointment, his/ her service will be terminated forthwith during probation period or even after confirmation of the service.
  • The Candidates whose postings are finalized after CBT, Verification and IME will be disqualified if they put external pressure for place of posting.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

RBI Assistant Salary 2026 offers an attractive compensation package for entry-level positions in the Reserve Bank of India, featuring a revised starting basic pay, various allowances like Dearness Allowance and House Rent Allowance, and strong job security with growth potential.

The exact in-hand amount varies by city of posting, current DA rates, and individual deductions, but it remains one of the most competitive clerical roles in the banking sector.

RBI Assistant Salary

RBI Assistant Salary in 2026 is attractive for a clerical-level post, with a revised basic pay of Rs. 29,000 per month and gross salary going up to around Rs. 58,514 per month, depending on the structure considered.

The role offers not just a steady income but also a sense of security, perks, and a clear growth path, which is why it stays on the radar of serious banking aspirants every year. Many candidates describe their first RBI salary credit as that moment when all the mock tests, late-night revisions, and cancelled plans finally feel worth it.

For 2026, the RBI Assistant salary is governed by a revised pay scale starting at Rs. 29,000, with regular increments built into the structure over the years. The in-hand salary generally comes slightly lower than the gross figure after deductions like NPS, taxes, and other small contributions, but it still remains competitive compared to most entry-level banking jobs.

The mix of respectable pay, a 5-day work week in RBI, and a more stable workload than many frontline private bank roles often makes this job feel like a long-term, sustainable career choice rather than just a stepping stone.

The RBI Assistant 2026 notification was released on February 16, 2026, for 650 vacancies (Panel Year 2025), with applications open from February 16 to March 8, 2026.

Reserve Bank of India RBI Assistant Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - APPLY NOW

RBI Assistant Salary Structure

RBI Assistant Salary structure in 2026 revolves around a revised basic pay of Rs. 29,000 and multiple allowances such as DA, HRA, grade allowance, and transport allowance added on top of it. Instead of looking at the salary as just one big figure, it helps to break it down into components, because that’s exactly what appears on the salary slip and what candidates usually Google right after getting selected.

As per the updated details, one structure shows a gross salary of Rs. 58,514 per month, depending on the combination of allowances considered. This difference mainly arises due to variation in specific allowance heads like additional pay, grade allowance, DA, and transport allowance.

Still, both point in the same direction: a solid entry-level banking salary where the fixed monthly earnings feel reliable enough to plan EMIs, savings, and the occasional weekend splurge.

RBI Assistant Pay Scale (as per latest pattern) The official pay scale is: ₹29,000 – 1700 (3) – 34,100 – 2040 (4) – 42,260 – 2720 (6) – 58,580 – 2950 (2) – 64,480 – 3370 (3) – 74,590 – 4050 (1) – 78,640.

  • Starting Basic Pay: ₹29,000 per month.
  • Increment Pattern: Annual increments as per the scale stages (e.g., ₹1,700 for first three years, then ₹2,040, etc.).
  • Maximum Basic Pay (end of scale): ₹78,640 (after years of service and increments).

This represents a significant revision from previous structures, enhancing entry-level earnings.

RBI Assistant Gross Salary - Component-Wise

The gross monthly emoluments (approximate, excluding HRA in some calculations) stand at around ₹58,514, including various allowances. Components are variable based on RBI rules, city classification, and current rates.

In practice, the exact figure may vary slightly with posting location and internal policies, but candidates can safely expect the gross to fall in this bracket when joining as RBI Assistant in 2026.

Check: RBI Assistant Syllabus and Exam Pattern 2026

RBI Assistant In-Hand Salary 2026 (After Deductions)

The in-hand (net) salary is the gross amount minus statutory and other deductions. Estimates range from approximately ₹48,000 to ₹55,000 per month for new joiners, depending on city, tax regime, and exact deductions.
Common Deductions

  • Provident Fund (PF) / National Pension System (NPS) contribution

  • Income Tax (TDS) – varies by individual slab/regime

  • Professional Tax (state-specific)

  • Other minor contributions (e.g., staff welfare, if applicable)

Net pay varies: higher in metros due to HRA but may include more tax; actual take-home is typically ₹50,000–₹54,000 in many cases (based on recent patterns). Individual factors like tax-saving investments can influence the final amount.

RBI Assistant Salary Deductions

RBI Assistant Salary deductions in 2026 mainly include contributions towards pension (like NPS or PF), professional tax, and a few small recurring deductions such as meal coupons or membership fees.
These are the things that make candidates think, “Why is the bank paying less than what the notification promised?” but they are standard for most government-linked jobs.

One detailed breakup shows that around Rs. 6,576 is deducted from the gross RBI Assistant salary every month under different heads.

This deduction brings the net in-hand salary slightly down from the gross number but still keeps it attractive for a fresher-level banking role. Most of these deductions are either statutory or linked to long-term benefits, so they quietly support future financial security even if they pinch a little in the first few months.

Deduction Head

Amount / Basis

EE NPS Contribution / PF

Around Rs. 4,941/-

Professional Tax

Around Rs. 200/-

Meal Coupon Deduction

Around Rs. 160/-

Around Rs. 225/-

Around Rs. 50/-

Consumer Article Loan (example)

Around Rs. 1,000/-

Other statutory deductions

As per rules (incl. IT/ESI where applicable)

Total Monthly Deductions

Around Rs. 6,576/- (Apx)

After these deductions, the in-hand salary often settles around the Rs. 52,000 – 55,000 range, depending on city category and specific structure.

RBI Assistant Allowances & Benefits 2026

Beyond salary, RBI Assistants enjoy comprehensive perks that add significant value:

  • House Rent Allowance (HRA): Paid if bank accommodation not provided; varies by city class (higher in X-class metros).

  • Dearness Allowance (DA): Inflation-linked; revised periodically for neutralisation.

  • Transport Allowance: Fixed support for commuting.

  • Medical Benefits: Reimbursement/coverage for self and dependents.

  • Leave & LTC: Eligible for leave travel concession and other leaves.

  • Retirement Benefits: Pension/gratuity under applicable rules, plus concessional loans and education allowances in some cases.

These benefits make the overall package highly rewarding compared to similar clerical roles.

RBI Assistant Job Profile

RBI Assistants handle clerical and support tasks in branches or offices. Daily work typically includes:

  • Data entry and record maintenance.

  • Assisting with customer queries and transactions.

  • Processing RTGS/NEFT/NEFT-related work.

  • Handling files, correspondence, and basic accounts.

  • Supporting departments like banking operations or currency management.

Office hours are generally standard (around 9–5), with reasonable work pressure compared to some commercial banks. It offers good work-life balance, making it a stable central banking career choice.

RBI Assistant Salary Slip

RBI Assistant salary slip is a monthly document that shows the detailed breakup of earnings and deductions, basic pay, allowances, contributions, and the final in-hand amount. It is the most realistic snapshot of what the RBI Assistant salary 2026 actually looks like in practice.

Typically, the slip will separately list basic pay, DA, HRA, transport allowance, grade allowance, special or local compensatory allowances, and then all deductions like NPS/PF, professional tax, and any other contributions. 

The final line shows the net salary credited to the account, which many candidates take a screenshot of and send to family or close friends the first time it arrives.

Even though sample slips may differ slightly between offices or states, the structure remains consistent and transparent, helping employees track their growth, understand tax slabs, and plan things like investments, insurance, or home loans.

Eventually, salary slips also become important proof for visa applications, loan processing, and future job switches, making them more than just a monthly PDF in the inbox.

RBI Assistant Salary in Different Cities / Posting Locations

Salary varies mainly due to HRA and CCA, which depend on whether the posting is in a metro, major city, or smaller center. Metro postings (like Mumbai, Delhi) often yield higher in-hand due to elevated allowances, while non-metro spots might be slightly lower but come with lower living costs.

RBI Assistant Salary Benefits

RBI Assistant salary benefits in 2026 go beyond the monthly cash component and include a wide range of perks, allowances, and facilities that add strong non-monetary value. For many employees, these benefits like accommodation, medical support, and concessional loans feel almost as important as the salary itself.

Some of the key benefits include:

  • Bank accommodation, subject to availability, with a very nominal license fee if allotted.

  • Reimbursement for vehicle maintenance used for official purposes.

  • Support for newspaper, briefcase, book grants, and furnishing of residence, as per eligibility.

  • Medical facilities provide dispensary support and reimbursement for OPD and hospitalization expenses.

  • Interest-free festival advances and Leave Fare Concession (LFC) for travel.

  • Concessional loans for housing, car, education, consumer goods, and even personal computers, after completing the required minimum service period.

  • Coverage under a defined contribution pension scheme, along with gratuity benefits.

There is also the intangible benefit of a 5-day work week in RBI and a more predictable work-life balance than many other banking jobs, which often becomes a deciding factor for people who value time with family or personal projects outside office hours.

Career Growth for RBI Assistant

Career growth for RBI Assistant in 2026 is structured yet rewarding, with clear paths to reach officer-level posts through internal exams and experience-based promotions. This is one of the main reasons many aspirants choose RBI Assistant over some private sector roles that may offer slightly higher initial pay but less predictable growth.

After completing the minimum required years of service usually about 2–3 years RBI Assistants become eligible to appear for departmental promotion processes. There are typically two main routes:

  • A normal process, where candidates clear a written test and are promoted based on seniority and performance.

  • A merit-based route, where additional qualifications like JAIIB and CAIIB along with experience play a big role.

Over time, an RBI Assistant can climb to posts such as Assistant Manager (Grade A), Manager (Grade B), Senior Manager (Grade C), and Chief Manager (Grade D), depending on performance and vacancies. 

Many long-term employees recall how the first promotion felt like a turning point: not just financially, but also in terms of confidence and responsibility. With consistent effort, exam preparation, and on-the-job learning, the RBI Assistant role can evolve from an entry point into a full-fledged central banking career.

RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 OUT - Download Hall Ticket at rbi.org.in

RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 has been released by the Reserve Bank of India on 2 April 2026. Candidates who applied for the Assistant recruitment (Panel Year 2025) can download their admit card from the official website opportunities.rbi.org.in or through the IBPS portal. The online preliminary examination is scheduled for 11 April 2026 and 13 April 2026 across various centers. This hall ticket is mandatory for appearing in the exam. Read below for the direct download link, step-by-step process, exam details, and important instructions for RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026.

How to Download RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official RBI website at www.rbi.org.in or go directly to opportunities.rbi.org.in
  2. Step 2: Navigate to the “Opportunities at RBI” or “Current Vacancies” section
  3. Step 3: Click on “Call Letters” or “Recruitment for the Post of Assistant – Panel Year 2025”
  4. Step 4: Select “Download Call Letter for Online Preliminary Examination”
  5. Step 5: Enter your credentials:
    • Registration Number / Roll Number
    • Password / Date of Birth
    • Captcha Code (if required)
  6. Step 6: Click on “Submit” button
  7. Step 7: Your RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen
  8. Step 8: Download and save the PDF file on your device
  9. Step 9: Take printout on A4 sheet with clear photograph

Pro Tip: Check all details on your admit card immediately after download. Report any discrepancies to RBI helpdesk without delay.

Details on RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026

Your RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Candidate Name
  • Roll Number / Registration Number
  • Date of Birth
  • Category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Gender
  • Candidate’s Photograph and Signature

Exam Information:

  • Exam Name: Assistant Prelims Online Exam
  • Exam Date & Shift/Timing
  • Reporting Time & Gate Closing Time
  • Exam Center Name and Complete Address
  • Language of Test

Important Instructions:

  • Documents to carry on exam day
  • Items allowed and not allowed in examination hall
  • Guidelines for PwBD candidates

Documents Required on RBI Assistant Prelims Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card (with photo)
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
    • Driving License
    • Voter’s ID Card
    • Bank Passbook with Photograph
    • Employee ID / College ID
  • Recent passport-size photographs (if required)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, Bluetooth devices
  • Calculators and electronic gadgets
  • Books, notes, study materials
  • Bags, purses, wallets

Note: Carrying prohibited items may lead to disqualification. Ration Card and Learner’s Driving License are not valid ID proofs.

Important Instructions for RBI Assistant Prelims Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card immediately and take multiple printouts
  • Verify exam center location and plan your travel
  • Check all details on the admit card for any errors
  • Read the Information Handout carefully

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center at least 45-60 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry will close as per gate closing time mentioned on admit card
  • Carry only allowed items; no safekeeping facility for prohibited items
  • Follow all COVID or other guidelines if mentioned

During Exam:

  • Negative marking of 0.25 for each wrong answer
  • Follow invigilator instructions strictly
  • Manage time for all sections
  • Do not carry any electronic devices inside the hall

What If RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card Not Available?

If you’re unable to download your RBI Assistant Prelims Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, try in incognito mode
  2. Solution 2: Use a different browser (Chrome recommended)

Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Selection Process 2026

The Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 selection process consists of multiple stages such as shortlisting, online assessment, psychometric evaluation, and personal interview. These stages are designed to select the most suitable candidates for wealth management roles.

Shortlisting & Application Scrutiny:

In the initial stage, applications will be scrutinised based on eligibility criteria, educational qualifications, relevant work experience, domain expertise, and other parameters decided by the bank. Only candidates shortlisted as per the bank’s internal norms will be called for further stages. Mere fulfilment of eligibility criteria does not guarantee shortlisting for the Bank of Baroda WMS selection process.

Online Test & Psychometric Assessment:

The selection process may include an online test followed by a psychometric assessment or other suitable evaluations. These tests are conducted to assess the candidate’s personality, communication skills, problem-solving ability, efficiency, innovation, and willingness to work anywhere in India. The bank reserves the right to conduct or waive the online test and/or psychometric test depending on recruitment requirements.

Personal Interview (PI):

Candidates shortlisted from earlier stages will be invited for a Personal Interview. During the interview, candidates will be evaluated on domain knowledge, relationship management skills, leadership qualities (especially for higher roles), communication skills, and overall suitability for wealth management positions. Candidates must secure a minimum of 60% marks (55% for reserved categories) or as decided by the bank.

Final Merit & Provisional Selection:

The final merit list will be prepared based on the candidate’s performance in the online test (if conducted), psychometric test, and personal interview, as per the weightage decided by the bank. Only those candidates who qualify in all stages and rank high in the merit list, subject to vacancy and category norms, will be provisionally selected. The decision of the bank in all matters related to selection will be final and binding.

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026

Interested and eligible candidates must apply online through the official website of Bank of Baroda within the specified dates. No other mode of application will be accepted.

Step 1 – Visit Official Website:

Visit the official website and navigate to the “Careers → Current Opportunities” section. Then click on the link for Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 (Advt. No. BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/08).

Step 2 – Read Detailed Notification:

Download and carefully read the official notification PDF to understand vacancy details, eligibility criteria, age limit, experience requirements, selection process, application fee, and instructions.

Step 3 – Online Registration:

Click on “Apply Online” and complete the registration by entering basic details such as name, mobile number, and email ID. Generate your registration ID and password. Ensure your contact details remain active throughout the recruitment process.

Step 4 – Fill Online Application Form:

Log in using your credentials and fill out the application form with accurate personal, educational, and professional details. Verify all information carefully before final submission, as no corrections will be allowed afterward.

Step 5 – Upload Photograph, Signature & Documents:

Upload a recent passport-size photograph, signature, and required documents such as educational certificates, experience letters, and category certificates in the prescribed format and size.

Step 6 – Pay Application Fee Online:

Pay the application fee through available online modes such as debit card, credit card, net banking, or UPI. After successful payment, download the e-receipt and application form.

Step 7 – Final Submission & Print:

Submit the application form and take a printout of the final submitted form and fee receipt for future reference, especially for interview and document verification.

General Instructions for Bank of Baroda WMS Recruitment 2026

Applicants for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 must adhere to all general instructions issued by the bank to ensure smooth processing of their applications and avoid disqualification at any stage.

Single Valid Application: Candidates should submit only one valid online application for a post; in case of multiple applications, only the latest complete application will be considered and all fees paid for previous registrations will stand forfeited. Multiple appearances for a single post in the selection process will lead to cancellation of candidature.

Accuracy of Information: All information furnished in the Bank of Baroda WMS online application form 2026 must be true, complete and correct. If any discrepancy, false information, or suppression of material facts is found at any stage (before or after appointment), the candidate’s candidature will be cancelled and, if already engaged, services may be terminated without notice.

Document Verification & Category Proof: Candidates must produce original documents (age proof, educational certificates, experience proofs, category certificates for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwD, NOC from employer, etc.) along with self‑attested photocopies at the time of interview or whenever called by the bank. No change of category will be permitted after online registration, and results will be processed based on the category declared in the application.

Email & Communication: All communication regarding Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 including call letters, schedule of tests/interviews, and any updates will be sent to the registered email ID and/or published on the Bank of Baroda website. The bank will not be responsible for non‑receipt of email due to incorrect email ID, spam filtering or technical issues.

Eligibility & Disqualification: Candidates are responsible for ensuring that they meet the eligibility criteria regarding nationality, age, qualification, experience and credit history. Applications from candidates with unsatisfactory credit reports, pending or adverse disciplinary/criminal records, or those not meeting the criteria at any stage are liable to be rejected and no correspondence in this regard will be entertained.

Jurisdiction & Bank’s Decision: Any dispute related to BOB WMS Recruitment 2026 will fall under the jurisdiction of courts/tribunals in Mumbai only. The decisions of Bank of Baroda regarding shortlisting, selection, eligibility, conduct of online test/PI and all other aspects of the recruitment process will be final, binding and not open to challenge by candidates.

Candidates can download the Provisional List of Selected Apprentices using the direct link. Check qualifying status, merit details, and next selection process below.

Are you waiting for PNB Apprentices Result 2026? Great news! Punjab National Bank has officially published the provisional result for Apprentices posts today (27th March 2026). Thousands of candidates who appeared in the online written examination held on 8th March 2026 can now verify their qualifying status online.

This article provides complete information about PNB Apprentices Result 2026 including direct download links, provisional selected list, expected next steps, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

How to Check PNB Apprentices Result 2026?

Candidates can check the PNB Apprentices Result 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website pnb.bank.in
  2. Click on the Recruitments / Careers section
  3. Look for the notice titled "27.03.2026 - Provisional List of Selected Apprentices as per Written Test on 08.03.2026"
  4. Download the PDF file
  5. Search your Roll Number / Name in the provisional selected list

PNB Apprentices Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The PNB Apprentices provisional list 2026 contains details of provisionally shortlisted candidates based on the online written test. Separate considerations may apply for categories.

Provisional List Contains:

  • Roll Numbers / Registration Numbers of shortlisted candidates
  • Candidate Names
  • Category details
  • Qualifying status for next stages

Next Steps After Provisional List:

  1. Local Language Test
  2. Medical Examination
  3. Document Verification
  4. Final Engagement as Apprentice

PNB Apprentices Scorecard / Result 2026 - Information Breakdown

The provisional result primarily shows shortlisting status. Individual scorecards (if released separately) would typically display:

Candidate Name As per application
Roll Number / Reg. No. Unique identification
Exam Date 8th March 2026

Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) Part-time Counsellor Notification 2026 has been released on the official website https://www.sebi.gov.in. A total vacancies have been announced through the SEBI Part-time Counsellor Recruitment 2026.

Candidates with no specific age limit who have a Post Graduate Degree in Clinical Psychology along with at least two years of post-qualification experience are eligible to apply for these Part-time Counsellor vacancies. The offline application process for SEBI Part-time Counsellor Recruitment 2026 is currently ongoing, and candidates must ensure that their application reaches the prescribed address on or before 11-05-2026.

Candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply offline through the direct links provided in this article.

Cosmos Co-operative Bank

Manager, General Manager and More Vacancy 2026

WWW.OnlyJobs

Application Fee

  • Refer Official Notification

Cosmos Co-operative Bank Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

  • Last Date for Apply Online: 14-04-2026

Cosmos Co-operative Bank Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

  • General Manager: Not more than 50 Yrs (52 Yrs for Experienced Candidates).
  • Deputy General Manager: Not more than 47 Yrs (50 Yrs for Experienced Candidates).
  • Assistant General Manager: Not more than 45 Yrs (48 Yrs for Experienced Candidates).
  • Chief Manager: Not more than 43 Yrs (46 Yrs for Experienced Candidates).
  • Manager: Not more than 40 Yrs (43 Yrs for Experienced Candidates).
  • Recovery Personnel: Not more than 30 Yrs (35 Yrs for Deserving / Experienced Candidates).
  • Fraud Risk Management Personnel: 30 - 40 Yrs (45 Yrs for Deserving / Experienced Candidates).
  • Age relaxation is applicable as per rules.

Qualification

  • General Manager: Graduate with 20 to 25 Yrs. in Co-op. Bank / Pvt. Bank / Nationalized Bank, out of which around 15 Yrs. in Executive Cadre is a must.
  • Deputy General Manager: Graduate with 15 to 20 Yrs. in Co-op. Bank / Pvt. Bank / Nationalized Bank, out of which around 8 to 10 Yrs. in Executive Cadre is a must.
  • Assistant General Manager: Graduate with CAIIB and / or CA/ CS/CMA/ CFA with 10 to 15 Yrs. in Co-op. Bank / Pvt. Bank / Nationalized Bank, out of which around 5 to 8 Yrs. in Executive Cadre is a must.
  • Chief Manager: Graduate with 8 to 10 Yrs. in Co-op. Bank / Pvt. Bank / Nationalized Bank, out of which around 5 Yrs. on Managerial Cadre is a must.
  • Manager: Graduate with CAIIB and / or CA/ CS/CMA/ CFA with 8 to 10 Yrs. in Co-op. Bank / Pvt. Bank / Nationalized Bank, out of which around 5 Yrs. in Asst. Managerial Cadre is a must.
  • Recovery Personnel: Graduate from any recognized University and LL.B. / LL.M. will be an added advantage, with 5 to 7 Yrs. Experience in Loan Collection, Credit / Recovery Handling.
  • Fraud Risk Management Personnel: Graduate from any recognized University with JAIIB / CAIIB an added advantage, with 5 to 10 Yrs. of Overall Banking Experience in Co-op. Bank / Pvt. Bank / Nationalized Bank out of which around 5 Yrs. in Fraud Risk Management / Monitoring is a must.

Remuneration

  • Commensurate with the Industry Standards.
