📄 Complete Details & Notification

RRB Group D Eligibility Criteria 2026 state that candidates must be Indian nationals (or as per specified rules) who have passed Class 10/ITI/NAC, are within the prescribed 18–33 years age limit (with relaxations for reserved categories), and meet the required medical and physical standards as detailed in the official notification.

The RRB Group D Notification 2026 (CEN 09/2025) defines these criteria for Level-1 posts such as Track Maintainer, Pointsman, Assistant Pointsman, Assistant Loco Shed, and others, with a total of around 22,195 vacancies (revised figures noted in some sources). The recruitment process includes a Computer-Based Test (CBT), Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Document Verification, and Medical Examination.

RRB Group D Eligibility Criteria 2026 – Quick Overview

Here are the key points at a glance:

  • Minimum Education: 10th pass (Matriculation) from a recognized board, or ITI from NCVT/SCVT, or National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in relevant trade.

  • Age Range: Generally 18–33 years (calculated as on the cutoff date mentioned in the notification, such as 01.01.2026).

  • Nationality Requirement: Primarily Indian citizens; certain other categories like subjects of Nepal/Bhutan or specified migrants are eligible with proper certificates.

  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Medical Standards: Mandatory qualifying stages after CBT.

  • Category-wise Age Relaxation: Available for OBC, SC/ST, Ex-servicemen, PwBD, and others as per rules.

Parameter

Details (as per 2026 notification)

Exam Name

RRB Group D Level 1 Posts 2026

Conducting Body

Railway Recruitment Board (RRB)

Education Required

10th pass / ITI / NAC from recognized institution

Age Limit (General)

18–33 years (as on cutoff date, e.g., 01.01.2026)

Nationality

Indian citizen or as per RRB rules

Physical Test

PET + medical fitness mandatory

Official Notification

CEN 09/2025 (RRB Group D Notification 2026)

Check the RRB Group D Notification 2026 PDF and Apply Online link below before applying (links available on official RRB websites like rrbapply.gov.in or regional portals during active periods).

RRB Group D Educational Qualification 2026

The minimum educational qualification for RRB Group D 2026 requires candidates to have:

  • Passed Class 10/Matriculation from a recognized board, or

  • ITI certificate from NCVT/SCVT in a relevant trade, or

  • National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) granted by NCVT.

Equivalence like NIOS/open schooling is generally accepted if recognized and mentioned in the official notification—always verify the exact clause in the CEN PDF. Final-year or appearing candidates are typically not eligible; the qualification must be completed by the application closing date.

Common doubts:

  • Gap years or backlogs do not disqualify if the certificate is obtained.

  • Subject stream usually does not matter for non-technical posts.

  • Always cross-check with the official notification to avoid issues.

RRB Group D Age Limit 2026

The general age limit for RRB Group D 2026 is 18 to 33 years, calculated as on the date specified in the notification (e.g., 01.01.2026). Candidates must fall within this range before applying.

Category

Minimum Age

Maximum Age (Base)

UR / General

18 years

33 years

OBC (NCL)

18 years

33 years + relaxation

SC / ST

18 years

33 years + relaxation

RRB Group D Age Relaxation 2026 (Category-wise)

RRB Group D age relaxation 2026 is provided over the upper age limit as follows (exact details in official CEN):

Category

Age Relaxation over Upper Age Limit

OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)

3 years

SC / ST

5 years

Ex-servicemen

As per length of service (generally 3–8 years or more)

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities)

10 years (additional for OBC/SC/ST)

Railway staff / Quasi-admin staff

As per norms and service length

Age relaxation is not automatic valid category certificates are mandatory at document verification. Confirm exact figures in the official CEN for 2026.

RRB Group D Nationality Criteria 2026

Under RRB Group D nationality criteria, candidates must be:

  • A citizen of India, or

  • A subject of Nepal or Bhutan, or

  • A Tibetan refugee who came to India before January 1, 1962 (with intent to settle permanently), or

  • A person of Indian origin migrated from specified countries (Pakistan, Burma, etc.) with an eligibility certificate from the Government of India.

Most applicants qualify as Indian citizens.

RRB Group D Physical Eligibility & PET 2026

The RRB Group D physical standards include two stages: PET (qualifying) and medical examination.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET) Requirements

PET is qualifying (pass/fail) and conducted for shortlisted CBT candidates. Typical requirements (as per notification; may vary slightly):

  • Male candidates: Running a specified distance in given time, lifting/carrying weight over distance.

  • Female candidates: Adjusted distance/weight parameters.

Only those passing PET proceed further.

RRB Group D Medical Standards 2026

The medical exam follows document verification and includes vision (distant/near), color vision, blood pressure, cardiac, hearing, and general fitness checks. Posts fall under A-2, B-1, C-1, etc., categories candidates must meet the standards for allotted posts.

Common disqualifying reasons include poor eyesight beyond limits, uncontrolled chronic conditions, or major disabilities incompatible with duties. Exact conditions are in the official rules.

Important Documents Required for RRB Group D Eligibility Verification

Candidates need these for various stages (especially document verification):

  • 10th/Matriculation certificate and mark sheet.

  • ITI/NAC certificate (if applicable).

  • Category certificates (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS in central format).

  • PwBD certificate (if claiming).

  • Ex-servicemen discharge certificate.

  • Photo ID (Aadhaar, Voter ID, etc.).

  • Passport-size photographs (per specs).

Who Is Not Eligible for RRB Group D 2026?

  • Candidates below 18 or above 33 years (even after relaxation).

  • Those without required 10th/ITI/NAC by cutoff date.

  • Non-eligible nationality or missing certificates.

  • Failing PET or medical exam.

  • Incomplete, incorrect, or forged documents.

  • Debarred by RRB or with disqualifying background (if specified).

How to Download RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 ?

Follow these simple steps to download your answer key:

  1. Visit the official website: www.rrbcdg.gov.in or respective regional RRB website
  2. Navigate to the "Answer Key" or "Recruitment" section
  3. Click on "RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 " link (CEN 02/2025: Exam Responses & Objection Tracker)
  4. Log in using your Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth
  5. Your answer key with recorded responses will appear on screen
  6. Download and save the PDF for future reference

How to Raise Objection Against RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 ?

If you disagree with any answer in the provisional key, follow these steps:

  1. Log in to your candidate portal on www.rrbcdg.gov.in or regional RRB site
  2. Go to the "Challenge/Objection" section (RRB Technician Objection Tracker)
  3. Select the question(s) you want to challenge
  4. Upload supporting documents or references (if required)
  5. Pay the objection fee (₹50 per question, refundable if accepted)
  6. Submit before the deadline: 27th March 2026 (10 am)

Important: Objections submitted after the deadline will NOT be considered.

RRB ALP Answer Key 2026 – Download PDF, Response Sheet & Objection Link

The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has released the RRB ALP Answer Key 2026 for CBT-1 (CEN 01/2025). Candidates who appeared for the Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) examination held on 13th, 16th, 17th, 18th February 2026 & Re-exam on 11th March 2026 can now check and download the provisional answer key, question paper, and response sheet from their respective regional RRB websites (access starts from 20th March 2026 at 5 PM IST).

This recruitment drive aims to fill 9970 vacancies. If you find any discrepancy in the answers, you can raise objections before 26th March 2026 (till 5 PM).

RRB ALP Answer Key 2026 – Quick Overview

RRB ALP Answer Key 2026
www.OnlyJobs
Organization Railway Recruitment Board (RRB)
Post Name Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP)
Total Vacancies 9970
Exam Date 13th, 16th, 17th, 18th February 2026 (Re-exam: 11th March 2026)
Answer Key Status Released
Release Date 20th March 2026 (from 5 PM IST)
Last Date for Objection 26th March 2026 (till 5 PM IST)
Official Website rrbcdg.gov.in (or respective regional RRB site)

Direct Link: View/Download RRB ALP Answer Key 2026, Response Sheet (Login Required) (Activate on 20 March 2026; use regional RRB portal)

How to Download RRB ALP Answer Key 2026 ?

Follow these simple steps to download your answer key:

  1. Visit the official regional RRB website (e.g., rrbcdg.gov.in or your zone's site)
  2. Navigate to the "CEN 01/2025" / "Answer Key" / "Recruitment" section
  3. Click on "RRB ALP Answer Key 2026 / View Question Paper, Response & Answer Key" link
  4. Log in using your Registration Number / Enrollment Number and Date of Birth / Password
  5. Your question paper, recorded responses, and provisional answer key will appear on screen
  6. Download and save the PDF for future reference

RRB ALP Answer Key 2026 – Important Links

Download Answer Key 2026 / View Response Sheet Click Here (Login Required)
Raise Objection Available in candidate login portal (during objection window)
Download Admit Card Check Regional RRB Site
Official Website rrbcdg.gov.in (Centralized info; check your regional RRB)
Download Mobile App
Join Telegram Channel
Join WhatsApp Channel

How to Raise Objection Against RRB ALP Answer Key 2026 ?

If you disagree with any answer in the provisional key, follow these steps:

  1. Log in to your candidate portal on the respective regional RRB website
  2. Go to the "Challenge/Objection" section (available during the window)
  3. Select the question(s) you want to challenge
  4. Upload supporting documents or references (if required)
  5. Pay the objection fee (₹ per question, refundable if accepted)
  6. Submit before the deadline: 26th March 2026 (5 PM IST)

Important: Objections submitted after the deadline will NOT be considered.

Event Date
Exam Conducted 13th, 16th, 17th, 18th February 2026 (Re-exam: 11th March 2026)
Provisional Answer Key Released 20th March 2026 (from 5 PM IST)
Last Date to Raise Objection 26th March 2026 (till 5 PM IST)
Final Answer Key (Expected) After objection review (TBA)
Result Declaration (Expected) To be announced soon

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

APPSC Answer Key 2026 - Download PDF, Response Sheet & Objection Link

The Andhra Pradesh Public Service Commission (APPSC) has released the Answer Key 2026 (Final Keys published on 09-04-2026) for posts including Assistant Inspector of Fisheries, Junior Lecturer (various subjects including Library Science), and Agriculture Officer. Candidates who appeared for the examinations held on 27-29 January 2026 can now check and download the answer keys from the official website portal-psc.ap.gov.in.

This recruitment drive aims to fill multiple vacancies across these posts. Final keys have been published for Assistant Inspector of Fisheries (Notification No. 23/2025) and Junior Lecturer in Library Science (Notification No. 14/2025), while Agriculture Officer (Notification No. 09/2025) initial keys were earlier released in February 2026. If you find any discrepancy, objections may be raised as per the portal guidelines (check current status on the site).

How to SC Answer Key 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your answer key:

  1. Visit the official website: portal-psc.ap.gov.in
  2. Navigate to the "Keys & Objections" or "Keys to Papers" section
  3. Click on the relevant "APPSC Answer Key 2026" link for your post (Assistant Inspector / Junior Lecturer / Agriculture Officer)
  4. Log in using your Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth (or view directly if PDF listed)
  5. Your answer key / response sheet will appear - download and save the PDF for future reference

How to Raise Objection Against APPSC Answer Key 2026?

If you disagree with any answer in the provisional key (where applicable):

  1. Log in to your candidate portal on portal-psc.ap.gov.in
  2. Go to the "Challenge/Objection" or "Keys & Objections" section
  3. Select the question(s) you want to challenge
  4. Upload supporting documents or references (if required)
  5. Pay the objection fee (if applicable, refundable if accepted)
  6. Submit before the deadline (check current status on portal)

Important: Objections submitted after the deadline will NOT be considered. Final keys for some posts were published on 09-04-2026 after earlier objection windows.
